project.name: course-dev-ide-with-agents
project.description: Collaborative intelligence infrastructure for course content production — deterministic manifest-compiled multi-agent orchestrator (Marcus-SPOC operator surface) with per-specialist 9-node scaffold, HIL gates, learning ledger, on LangChain/LangGraph.

nodes.name: elevenlabs_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Importlib dispatch seam loading the legacy skills-tree elevenlabs_operations.py module (with package stubs for its expected import context) and dispatching allow-listed Enrique operation modes to it, coercing provider API errors into tagged dispatch errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Injects synthetic module stubs into sys.modules so the file-loaded legacy script resolves its package-relative imports outside its home tree.

nodes.name: _load_operations_module
nodes.summary: Loads the legacy elevenlabs_operations.py from the skills tree via importlib file-location spec after installing package stubs.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_elevenlabs
nodes.summary: Validates the requested mode against the Enrique allow-list, invokes the corresponding legacy operation, and wraps API failures into tagged ElevenlabsDispatchError records.

nodes.name: narration_join.py
nodes.summary: Single canonical home of the narration join policy (narration_script joined with segment-manifest deltas by segment id) shared by the storyboard-B publisher, Enrique's audio synthesis, and Quinn-R's G5 pre-composition QA so all three validate the same join. Pure functions with no I/O; starvation handling is left to callers.
nodes.languageNotes: An import-identity pin enforces that all three consumers route through this module rather than re-implementing the join.

nodes.name: join_narration_segments
nodes.summary: Joins Pass-2 narration script rows with segment-manifest deltas by segment id, resolving each row's slide_id from the first non-empty visual_reference perception source.

nodes.name: phantom_segment_ids
nodes.summary: Returns segment ids present in the joined rows that have no narration text, flagging phantom segments for caller-side starvation handling.

nodes.name: collapsed_segment_ids
nodes.summary: Identifies segment ids whose join collapsed multiple deltas into one row, so callers can detect lost granularity in the narration-to-slide mapping.

nodes.name: marcus_interlocutor.py
nodes.summary: The LLM-driven stop-and-chat interlocution REPL (S5): at each paused gate Marcus narrates the real decision card in persona, answers free-form operator turns via the injected chat model, and only after a deterministic guard validates the verb against the gate's allowed set does it confirm and drive resume_production_trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Chat model and I/O are dependency-injected seams: offline tests inject a fake BaseChatModel and scripted input, and any live model call in the offline path raises rather than silently falling through.

nodes.name: StructuredAction
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for the LLM's proposed gate action (verb plus payload) with a parse method that extracts the structured action from a model turn.

nodes.name: ValidatedDecision
nodes.summary: A guard-approved gate decision carrying the validated verb and payload plus a confirm_echo rendering shown to the operator before the engine is driven.

nodes.name: MarcusInterlocutor
nodes.summary: The REPL engine: holds the injected chat model, grounding context, and I/O seams; per gate it narrates the decision card, loops operator turns through the LLM, validates and confirms decisions, drives the resume, and persists the transcript.

nodes.name: validate_action_against_gate
nodes.summary: The load-bearing deterministic guard: checks a proposed verb and selection against the gate's allowed verb set before any engine drive, returning a ValidatedDecision or GuardRefusal.

nodes.name: run_marcus_interlocutor
nodes.summary: Public entry that constructs a MarcusInterlocutor for a paused trial and runs the conversation loop to completion or clean error-pause.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Interactive CLI entrypoint wiring real stdin/stdout, live chat model, freshly computed overlay staleness, and runtime preconditions into the interlocutor.

nodes.name: marcus_spoc.py
nodes.summary: Marcus SPOC — the conversational single-point-of-contact operator surface over the production engine: at each gate it narrates the relevant content in persona (sources, ingestion, lesson plan, research, treatment, voice, G0 enrichment), runs the styleguide picker preflight and pick-commit ceremony, collects per-gate decisions, and drives production_runner under the hood.
nodes.languageNotes: The deterministic orchestration engine remains production_runner; this module is the operator-facing narration layer, with decisions supplied as a gate_id-to-verb map (scripted or operator-in-the-loop).

nodes.name: _CeremonyBudget
nodes.summary: Small attempt-budget tracker for the picker confirm ceremony, consuming attempts until exhausted.

nodes.name: narrate_kickoff_beat1
nodes.summary: Renders Marcus's opening kickoff narration for a run: the beat-1 in-persona framing of what is about to be produced and from which sources.

nodes.name: _last_pick_for_course
nodes.summary: Scans the styleguide pick-event ledger for the most recent pick matching the course key, powering the pick recommendation narration.

nodes.name: narrate_research_result
nodes.summary: Narrates the research contribution outcome in persona, including degraded-mode disclosure when retrieval credentials were absent.

nodes.name: narrate_picker_publish_degrade
nodes.summary: Narrates a picker gh-pages publish failure honestly (with scrubbed error detail) and pivots the operator to the inline numbered fallback.

nodes.name: decode_and_echo_pick
nodes.summary: Decodes a pasted picker selection code against the roster and echoes the resolved style guide back to the operator for confirmation.

nodes.name: commit_picker_pick
nodes.summary: Commits a confirmed styleguide pick: validates it is pickable, writes the pick into the run directive, and appends the pick event to the ledger.

nodes.name: _confirm_codes
nodes.summary: Runs the confirm sub-loop of the picker ceremony: reads operator replies, distinguishes confirm/reject/re-paste, and enforces the ceremony budget.

nodes.name: _paste_confirm_commit_loop
nodes.summary: The paste-confirm-commit ceremony for the published picker page: accepts a pasted selection code, echoes the decode, and commits on operator confirmation.

nodes.name: _inline_numbered_pick
nodes.summary: Inline numbered-list fallback for styleguide selection when the published picker page is unavailable.

nodes.name: _degraded_publish_pick
nodes.summary: Full degraded-path pick flow when picker publish fails: narrates the degrade, offers the inline numbered list, and still routes the confirmed pick through the same commit path.

nodes.name: run_picker_preflight
nodes.summary: Pre-start styleguide picker ceremony: loads the roster, surfaces a last-pick recommendation, publishes the picker page (or degrades to inline pick), and commits the operator's confirmed choice into the directive.

nodes.name: run_marcus_spoc
nodes.summary: Main SPOC loop: starts the production trial, then at each gate pause narrates the decision card, resolves the mapped (or default approve) decision, and resumes production_runner until completion.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: BMAD skill definition activating the sensory bridges agent persona with workflow instructions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: BMAD skill definition activating the source wrangler agent persona with workflow instructions.

nodes.name: refresh-report-elevenlabs.json
nodes.summary: Tech-spec wrangler refresh report capturing ElevenLabs API capability drift findings.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: BMAD skill definition activating the tech spec wrangler agent persona with workflow instructions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: BMAD skill definition activating the woodshed agent persona with workflow instructions.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Enrique (ElevenLabs audio) specialist LangGraph: wires the standard 9-node scaffold (receive through handoff) around Enrique's act implementation, loading sanctum digest and skill references and binding the gate-decision interrupt to the resume API.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Enrique consumed-payload contract: a frozen set of every envelope payload key Enrique's act body reads, asserted by the Ratchet-D manifest lockstep test to prevent silently ungrounded audio dispatches.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Gary's Gamma slide-generation act implementation: normalizes styleguide-resolved Gamma API settings, generates classic and studio deck variants (with per-slide studio image cards), and emits styleguide parity receipts and fidelity cohorts for downstream gating.
nodes.languageNotes: Setting normalization is a single large deterministic pass (~240 lines) so that classic/studio surface-key validation and enum checks live in one auditable place.

nodes.name: gamma_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Thin dispatch seam between Gary and the Gamma generation backend: routes to the live gamma_operations executor or a deterministic fixture receipt, raising GammaDispatchError on pre-receipt failures.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Gary gamma-generation specialist LangGraph (Story 2b.1): 9-node scaffold that decodes the envelope payload, records dispatch-trail entries, parses Gamma dispatch receipts, and binds the gate-decision interrupt to the resume API.

nodes.name: learned_dependencies.py
nodes.summary: Hermetic learned-dependencies store for the CD-owned Gamma styleguide: an append-only digest-idempotent JSONL observation ledger plus manifest-pinned promoted YAML rules, applied offline to Gamma settings with predicate evaluation and pin verification.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately file-based (not the Postgres event ledger) so rule validation stays offline and hermetic; observations are deduplicated by content digest.

nodes.name: styleguide_library.py
nodes.summary: Byte-compatible barrel re-export of the shared Gamma styleguide resolver (app.styleguide.resolver), preserving Gary's full prior public API so existing import sites keep working after the strangler-fig extraction.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Irene specialist package barrel: re-exports build_irene_graph plus the IreneEnvelope and IreneReturn state types.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the Irene Pass-2 authoring contract: re-exports the Pydantic envelope, segment-manifest, voice-direction, and perception-projection surface from pass_2_template.

nodes.name: pass_2_template.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic source of truth for Irene Pass-2 authoring guidance: strict models for Gary slide output, perception artifacts, segment manifests, voice direction, and the top-level IrenePass2AuthoringEnvelope with cross-artifact shape validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Path validators avoid negative lookahead (unsupported by Pydantic v2's Rust regex) by rejecting colons outside a Windows drive prefix — an operator-ratified trade-off.

nodes.name: build_enrique_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles Enrique's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph, registering the scaffold node sequence and edges from START to END.

nodes.name: decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates the JSON envelope payload dispatched to Gary, raising GaryActError on malformed input.

nodes.name: _normalized_gamma_settings
nodes.summary: Normalizes the raw styleguide-resolved settings into validated Gamma API options: theme resolution, enum validation, classic-only surface-key enforcement, and studio overrides, in one deterministic pass.

nodes.name: _instructions_for_variant
nodes.summary: Composes the additional-instructions string for a deck variant by layering source-derived instructions on top of the styleguide base instructions.

nodes.name: _assert_studio_image_card
nodes.summary: Asserts that a generated studio card satisfies the per-slide image contract, raising with diagnostic detail when the card shape is invalid.

nodes.name: _generate_studio_variant
nodes.summary: Generates the studio deck variant via per-slide single-card Gamma calls, validating each image card and accumulating per-slide receipts.

nodes.name: _styleguide_parity_receipt
nodes.summary: Builds the styleguide parity receipt by comparing Gary's local resolution against the shared resolver's canonical digest, recording classification and crash detail for audit.

nodes.name: _classic_generation_kwargs
nodes.summary: Assembles the keyword arguments for a classic (non-studio) Gamma generation call from the normalized settings and variant-specific text, image, and card options.

nodes.name: generate_gamma_variants
nodes.summary: Core generation loop: produces classic and studio Gamma deck variants from the envelope payload, applies learned rules and parity receipts, partitions binary-fidelity cohorts, and returns the full dispatch receipt bundle.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Gary's act entry point: decodes the envelope, runs generate_gamma_variants, and shapes the specialist return payload with trail entries and receipts.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_gamma
nodes.summary: Dispatches a generation request to the Gamma backend via the gamma_operations executor, falling back to a deterministic fixture receipt in non-billing/dev paths and wrapping failures in GammaDispatchError.

nodes.name: _parse_dispatch_receipt
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the Gamma dispatch receipt structure returned by the act node, raising ReceiptParseError with diagnostics when required fields are missing or malformed.

nodes.name: build_gary_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles Gary's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold node sequence and gate-decision interrupt wiring.

nodes.name: apply_learned_rules
nodes.summary: Applies active promoted learned rules to a Gamma settings dict, evaluating each rule's predicate and recording which rules fired for the receipt trail.

nodes.name: predicate_hash
nodes.summary: Computes a stable content digest of a rule predicate for idempotent ledger deduplication and manifest pinning.

nodes.name: read_observations
nodes.summary: Reads the append-only JSONL observation ledger from disk, returning parsed observation records.

nodes.name: append_observation
nodes.summary: Appends a new observation to the JSONL ledger, digest-idempotently skipping records already present so re-runs stay byte-identical.

nodes.name: load_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads the learned-rules lock manifest (gamma-learned-rules.lock) used to pin promoted rule identity.

nodes.name: check_manifest_pin
nodes.summary: Validates promoted rules against the identity-manifest pin so a rule cannot silently vanish or mutate: verifies digests, provenance citations, and lock coverage, returning structured failure detail.

nodes.name: active_learned_rules
nodes.summary: Returns the currently active promoted learned rules after manifest-pin verification, the safe read surface consumed by Gary's act path.

nodes.name: GarySlideOutput
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model of a single Gary-produced slide artifact, validating that the file path is a PNG.

nodes.name: PerceptionArtifact
nodes.summary: Minimal authoring-envelope projection of the rich upstream perception artifact — template/validator-compatible but not a runtime grounding authority.

nodes.name: ElevenLabsSettings
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of ElevenLabs TTS synthesis settings (stability, similarity, style, speed) carried in the voice-direction contract.

nodes.name: DialogueTurn
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of a single dialogue turn within a narration segment, with speaker and text fields.

nodes.name: VoiceDirection
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of delivery-metadata voice direction (tone, pacing, emphasis, TTS defaults) attached to frozen segments; coerces sequences to tuples for immutability.

nodes.name: SegmentManifestSegment
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of one segment-manifest row (segment id, narration text, visual file, behavioral intent), normalizing the visual file reference.

nodes.name: IrenePass2AuthoringEnvelope
nodes.summary: Top-level strict Pydantic envelope for Irene Pass-2 authoring: validates UTC-aware generation timestamps, complete procedural rules, and cross-artifact shape consistency between slides, perception artifacts, and segment manifests.

nodes.name: project_rich_perception_for_authoring
nodes.summary: Projects the rich vision-produced perception artifact down to the minimal authoring-envelope PerceptionArtifact shape for template and validator compatibility.

nodes.name: voice_direction_annotation.py
nodes.summary: Pure, deterministic post-freeze voice-direction annotation pass (P5 Step 2): layers voice_direction delivery metadata onto an already-frozen, figure-gate-passed segment manifest without touching any grounded field, so delivery intent can never leak into word choice.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately a separate pure pass (no I/O, no LLM, no clock, no randomness) rather than part of the LLM Pass-2 call; segments are deep-copied so caller input is never mutated.

nodes.name: warm_callback.py
nodes.summary: Pure deterministic safety core for warm callbacks: computes the grounded anchor denominator (strictly-prior teachable parent components) and enforces the Vera-R7 block-by-omission gate so an ungrounded callback is dropped whole rather than stripped.

nodes.name: warm_callback_authoring.py
nodes.summary: Generative half of the warm-callback feature: default-OFF env flag plus an injectable renderer seam whose prompt structurally forbids figures, negation, and comparators so polarity reversal is unreachable by construction.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Irene Pass-2 specialist graph: the largest specialist module, assembling the byte-stable Pass-2 narration prompt, invoking the LLM, and enforcing an extensive grounding regime — figure-fidelity assertions, reading-path conformance, slide-key joins, warm-callback attachment, and voice-direction annotation — before the gate-decision interrupt.
nodes.languageNotes: Prompt assembly is pinned byte-stable (sorted POSIX paths, CRLF-normalized reads, canonical json.dumps, no datetimes/UUIDs) so cache prefixes are deterministic across platforms.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Irene Pass-2 consumed-payload contract: the frozen set of envelope keys the Pass-2 act body reads, asserted by the Ratchet-D manifest lockstep test to prevent ungrounded-prompt regressions.

nodes.name: cluster_floor.py
nodes.summary: Cluster-floor enforcement that honors the minimum cluster floor by grouping, seaming, and splitting plan clusters and records floor consumption.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Irene Pass-1 9-node scaffold LangGraph wiring the standard receive-through-handoff node sequence around the Pass-1 act implementation with a gate-decision interrupt.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Kim specialist 9-node scaffold graph: parses the operator-instructions envelope, assembles CourseArc instructions in the act node, and follows the standard receive-through-handoff sequence with gate interrupt and resume binding.

nodes.name: annotate_segments_with_voice_direction
nodes.summary: The annotation pass itself: deep-copies each frozen segment and attaches a voice_direction resolved by the pinned precedence (per-segment override, contribution overlay, defaults), refusing to modify any grounded field and reporting offending locations on violation.

nodes.name: select_grounded_callback_anchors
nodes.summary: Computes the grounding denominator for warm callbacks: the strictly-prior teachable parent component_ids a callback may legitimately reference, from compute_teaches_after intersected with derive_teachable; returns an empty tuple when nothing qualifies.

nodes.name: WarmCallbackGateResult
nodes.summary: Result dataclass for the warm-callback gate: kept flag plus the containment audit trail the caller records.

nodes.name: gate_warm_callback
nodes.summary: The Vera-R7 gate with teeth: runs rhetorical-source containment and the no-new-figure check against the frozen figure_tokens neck; a failure returns kept=False so the caller drops the whole callback (strip is forbidden).

nodes.name: warm_callback_authoring_active
nodes.summary: Default-OFF environment flag check for warm-callback authoring; when off, Pass-2 output is byte-unchanged.

nodes.name: build_warm_callback_render_messages
nodes.summary: Builds the renderer prompt messages that constrain the callback to reference the prior concept only — no numerals, negation, or comparators — making polarity reversal unreachable by construction (AC6).

nodes.name: render_warm_callback
nodes.summary: Injectable renderer seam: invokes the supplied model callable (real chat-invoke in production, deterministic fake in tests) with the constrained prompt and returns the rendered callback text.

nodes.name: narration_figure_fidelity_active
nodes.summary: Environment-flag check controlling whether the narration figure-fidelity grounding regime is enforced during Pass 2.

nodes.name: _slide_roster
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical slide roster from Gary slide output and lesson-plan units, establishing the slide-id join surface every downstream grounding assertion validates against.

nodes.name: backfill_delta_ids
nodes.summary: Backfills missing segment-manifest delta identifiers deterministically so downstream consumers (Enrique's audio joins) always see complete, joinable delta rows.

nodes.name: backfill_delta_perception_sources
nodes.summary: Backfills perception-source references onto segment-manifest deltas from the per-slide perception artifacts so each delta carries its visual evidence provenance.

nodes.name: assert_callback_reading_path
nodes.summary: Asserts injected warm callbacks do not break the VO-to-on-screen reading-path conformance of the segment they were attached to.

nodes.name: assert_pass2_surfaces_validatable
nodes.summary: Pre-flight assertion that all Pass-2 output surfaces (roster, deltas, canonical fields) are present and shaped so the grounding gate suite can actually validate them, failing loud instead of silently skipping.

nodes.name: _attach_warm_callbacks
nodes.summary: Warm-callback attachment pipeline: selects grounded anchors, renders the callback via the injectable seam, runs the Vera-R7 gate, injects passing callbacks into the canonical segment fields, and restamps rhetorical-role keys — dropping any callback that fails containment.

nodes.name: _assemble_pass_2_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the byte-stable Pass-2 narration prompt from sanctum files, skill references, redacted payload sections, perceived-figure blocks, and source-figure authority — deterministic for cache-prefix stability (NFR-I6).

nodes.name: _act_pass_1
nodes.summary: Legacy Pass-1 act path inside the Irene graph: assembles the Pass-1 prompt, invokes the chat model, and parses the plan response.

nodes.name: _attach_voice_direction
nodes.summary: Post-freeze wiring of the voice-direction annotation pass: resolves defaults, overrides, and role-derived per-slide seeds from the payload and applies annotate_segments_with_voice_direction to the frozen segment manifest when the flag is active.

nodes.name: _act_pass_2
nodes.summary: The Pass-2 act body: assembles the prompt, invokes the LLM, parses the response, backfills delta ids and perception sources, runs the full grounding assertion suite, attaches warm callbacks and voice direction, and stamps slide keys.

nodes.name: build_irene_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles Irene's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold node sequence and gate-decision interrupt wiring.

nodes.name: unit_source_refs
nodes.summary: Extracts the verbatim source_refs anchor strings from a plan unit for seam-legitimacy analysis.

nodes.name: classify_anchor_role
nodes.summary: Deterministically classifies a source anchor into the fixed role vocabulary {figure, narration, claim} from image/file markers, frozen figure-token numeral hits, or narration markers.

nodes.name: group_plan_clusters
nodes.summary: Groups the flat plan_units list into clusters as contiguous runs sharing a cluster_id.

nodes.name: _run_seams
nodes.summary: Enumerates candidate split seams within a cluster run, deriving each seam's legitimacy from adjacent units' anchor roles and bond rules.

nodes.name: _split_run
nodes.summary: Splits a cluster run at chosen seams: promotes the trailing part's first unit to head of a newly minted cluster and recounts interstitials, reshaping boundaries only in the existing vocabulary.

nodes.name: honor_min_cluster_floor
nodes.summary: Enforces the minimum cluster floor by grouping, seaming, and splitting plan clusters.

nodes.name: consume_min_cluster_floor
nodes.summary: Consumes the min_cluster_floor runner-context key from the payload, validates its configuration, runs honor_min_cluster_floor, and returns a FloorConsumption receipt.

nodes.name: assert_floor_consulted
nodes.summary: Post-hoc assertion that the floor pass was actually consulted for the run, guarding against a dead-config floor silently skipped (DeadFloorConfigError).

nodes.name: FloorConsumption
nodes.summary: Receipt dataclass recording how the min-cluster floor was consumed: floor value, cluster counts before/after, and split decisions.

nodes.name: build_irene_pass1_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles the Irene Pass-1 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold transitions and gate-decision interrupt.

nodes.name: _parse_operator_instructions_envelope
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the operator-instructions envelope payload delivered to Kim, raising OperatorInstructionsParseError with detail on malformed input.

nodes.name: _act
nodes.summary: Kim's act node: decodes the envelope, parses operator instructions, and assembles the CourseArc instruction output with a trail entry.

nodes.name: build_kim_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles Kim's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold transitions and gate-decision interrupt.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Kira's Kling API act implementation: loads the per-slide motion plan (from payload dict or motion_plan.yaml), enforces budget and consumed-payload keys, calls the Kling client to generate video per slide, and writes receipts and inspection notes to the run bundle.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Kira motion specialist LangGraph (Story 2a.3): 9-node scaffold wiring the Kling act implementation with sanctum digest, kling skill references, gate-decision interrupt, and resume binding.

nodes.name: kling_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Thin dispatch seam for Kira motion generation: returns a deterministic fixture MP4 by default (non-billing dev path) and dynamically loads the kling-video skill's run_motion_generation module when a real motion-plan context is provided.
nodes.languageNotes: Loads the skill script via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location rather than a package import, keeping the skills tree decoupled from the app package.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Kira consumed-payload contract: frozen set of envelope keys (motion_plan, motion_plan_path, slides, bundle_path, upstream_output) declared so the Ratchet-D manifest-vocabulary test can audit the motion data-plane edge.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Mira specialist 9-node scaffold graph: parses the operator-instructions envelope and assembles a prompt pack in the act node, following the standard receive-through-handoff sequence with gate interrupt and resume binding.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Motion-planner (07D.5) act: a deterministic, LLM-free adapter over the Epic-14 motion engine that reads the authorized winner deck, applies or auto-derives motion designations, and projects designated slides into Kira's motion_plan shape with catalog-fused prompts, validated model ids, and cost estimates.
nodes.languageNotes: Binding amendment A pins 'no model client touched here' as a test invariant; the kling-video references library is the SSOT for prompt templates and valid model ids.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Motion-plan producer specialist LangGraph (07D.5): 9-node scaffold where plan() records the model-resolution trail for uniformity but act() is purely deterministic — no LLM in the critical path.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Motion-planner consumed-payload contract: frozen set of envelope keys (upstream_output, authorized_storyboard, motion_designations, perception_artifacts, bundle_path) validated by the Ratchet-D manifest-edge test for the 07D.5 dependency edge.

nodes.name: decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates Kira's JSON envelope payload against the consumed-payload contract, raising KiraActError on malformed input.

nodes.name: _load_motion_plan
nodes.summary: Resolves the motion plan from the payload's explicit motion_plan dict, a motion_plan_path YAML file, or the legacy top-level slides list, in that priority order.

nodes.name: _call_generate_motion
nodes.summary: Invokes the Kling client to generate a single slide's motion video, handling the polling/response cycle and wrapping backend failures.

nodes.name: generate_motion_from_plan
nodes.summary: Main motion-generation loop: iterates the plan's slides sequentially, checks the budget, calls Kling per slide, records per-slide receipts, video URLs, and inspection notes under the run bundle's motion directory.

nodes.name: build_compositor_invocation
nodes.summary: Builds the downstream compositor invocation record pointing at the generated motion assets.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Kira's act entry point: decodes the envelope, runs generate_motion_from_plan, and shapes the specialist return payload with trail entries.

nodes.name: _read_sanctum_digest
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic digest over Kira's sanctum files for prompt provenance and cache-prefix stability.

nodes.name: build_kira_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles Kira's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold node sequence and gate-decision interrupt wiring.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_kling
nodes.summary: Dispatches motion generation: returns the deterministic fixture MP4 when no runtime motion-plan context is provided, otherwise loads and runs the kling-video skill's real generation backend for the given slide, wrapping failures in KlingDispatchError.

nodes.name: _parse_operator_instructions_envelope
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the operator-instructions envelope payload delivered to Mira, raising OperatorInstructionsParseError with detail on malformed input.

nodes.name: _act
nodes.summary: Mira's act node: decodes the envelope, parses operator instructions, and assembles the prompt-pack output with a trail entry.

nodes.name: build_mira_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles Mira's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold transitions and gate-decision interrupt.

nodes.name: decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates the motion-planner's JSON envelope payload against the consumed-payload contract, raising MotionPlannerActError on malformed input.

nodes.name: _resolve_authorized_storyboard
nodes.summary: Resolves the authorized winner storyboard from the quinn_r upstream_output payload or an explicit authorized_storyboard dict (dev/replay path), validating the slide structure it yields.

nodes.name: _project_slides
nodes.summary: Projects the video/animation-designated Epic-14 recommendation rows into Kira's motion_plan slide shape, fusing motion briefs with catalog prompt templates and attaching cost estimates.

nodes.name: build_motion_plan
nodes.summary: Builds the full deterministic motion plan: runs the Epic-14 engine over the authorized storyboard, applies provided or auto-derived designations, selects styles from the video-style catalog, validates model ids against model-capabilities, and enforces the credit budget.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Motion-planner act entry point: decodes the envelope, resolves the storyboard, builds the motion plan, and returns the specialist payload with trail entries — no model client touched.

nodes.name: build_motion_planner_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles the motion-planner's 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with the scaffold transitions and gate-decision interrupt.

nodes.name: classify_fidelity_reference
nodes.summary: Classifies a narration text span as fidelity-bearing (references visuals or figures) or non-visual.

nodes.name: build_compositor_invocation
nodes.summary: Builds the compositor invocation payload referencing the generated audio beds.

nodes.name: canonical_resolution_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the sha256 digest over the canonical JSON serialization of a resolved styleguide mapping, shared byte-for-byte by CD emission and Gary's shadow comparison.

nodes.name: sme_registry.py
nodes.summary: Per-SME voice/styleguide/attribution resolution layer over state/config/sme-registry.yaml; unknown SME keys hard-fail and unbound SMEs return marked gaps rather than silently inheriting another styleguide.
nodes.languageNotes: Registry loading is lru_cache-memoized with an explicit clear_sme_registry_cache escape hatch for tests.

nodes.name: SmeProfile
nodes.summary: Pydantic profile for one SME: display name, voice/styleguide bindings, attribution, and approval fields with non-empty validation.

nodes.name: load_sme_registry
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the SME registry YAML into an SmeRegistry model, memoized per path.

nodes.name: resolve_sme_key
nodes.summary: Resolves a free-form SME name or key to a canonical registry key, hard-failing on unknown SMEs with the available key set in the error.

nodes.name: resolve_sme_profile
nodes.summary: Resolves an SME name to its full profile, returning marked gap fields for unbound SMEs so callers never silently fall back to a default styleguide.

nodes.name: profiles_diverge
nodes.summary: Field-by-field comparison of two SME profiles reporting which binding fields diverge.

nodes.name: prose_uplift.py
nodes.summary: Prose-uplift pass that rewrites deictic narration into standalone workbook prose and measures the reduction delta in deixis and unresolved markers.

nodes.name: ProseUpliftDelta
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing measurable prose-uplift results (revoice markers cleared, deixis reduced) with a to_dict serialization for audit reporting.

nodes.name: measure_prose_delta
nodes.summary: Computes a ProseUpliftDelta by comparing revoice-marker and deixis counts between original and uplifted text.

nodes.name: uplift_narration_text
nodes.summary: Rewrites deictic narration into standalone workbook prose and reports the uplift delta.

nodes.name: sme_aware_prose_revoicer
nodes.summary: Builds a prose revoicer callable that resolves the SME profile from the registry and applies attribution-aware uplift, for injection into the workbook producer.

nodes.name: RuntimePrecondition
nodes.summary: Model of a runtime blocker (e.g., missing scite OAuth token) that keeps a wired capability from actually running right now.

nodes.name: CapabilityFact
nodes.summary: One specialist's capability fact (state, rationale, routed nodes) with an honest_summary rendering that never up-levels the state.

nodes.name: load_overlay
nodes.summary: Loads the S4-generated capability-overlay YAML mapping from disk.

nodes.name: run_gate_decide
nodes.summary: Validates a verdict payload into OperatorVerdict, resolves the resume command via resume_from_verdict, and wraps it in a CLI transport response.

nodes.name: _system_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds Marcus's persona system prompt anchored on the capability grounding context.

nodes.name: _normalize_input_source
nodes.summary: Normalizes the injected operator input seam (callable, iterable, or interactive input) into a uniform line-yielding callable.

nodes.name: _adhoc_trace_env
nodes.summary: Derives the LangSmith tracing environment context for ad-hoc facade asks.

nodes.name: _resolve_activation_allowlist
nodes.summary: Resolves which agent activations the facade permits, guarding the surface against unexpected routing.

nodes.name: get_bundle
nodes.summary: Looks up a BundleRecord by its ratified bundle id from the frozen catalog.

nodes.name: _validate_bundle_collateral_pair
nodes.summary: Validates bundle collateral pair for the lesson planning flow.

nodes.name: load_lesson_plan_collateral_selection
nodes.summary: Loads lesson plan collateral selection for the lesson planning flow.

nodes.name: run_asset_index.py
nodes.summary: UDAC v1 Run Asset Index (RAI): the neutral access-and-use contract with canonical digesting, typed legacy modes, fail-loud loaders, and asset resolution to a read-only view — placed models-side so both the orchestrator (builder) and specialists (consumers) can import it without violating the M3 layering contract.
nodes.languageNotes: RESOLVE-ASSET-LIVES-NEUTRAL discipline: this module imports no app.marcus.orchestrator module so specialists can consume the resolved view without a layering violation.

nodes.name: RunAssetEntry
nodes.summary: One RAI entry: asset id, path, digest, authority status, and declared usage.

nodes.name: ResolvedAsset
nodes.summary: Read-only resolved view of an asset handed to consuming specialists.

nodes.name: RunAssetIndex
nodes.summary: The Run Asset Index built by the orchestrator at ratification gates and consumed by specialists as the single access contract.

nodes.name: recompute_digest_from_disk
nodes.summary: Recomputes an asset digest from disk, honoring the typed legacy mode and digest algorithm.

nodes.name: compare_digests
nodes.summary: Compares recorded versus recomputed digests, classifying match, mismatch, and legacy outcomes.

nodes.name: resolve_asset
nodes.summary: Neutral asset resolution: validates authority and digest and returns a ResolvedAsset; raises AssetResolutionError on any contract breach.

nodes.name: load_rai
nodes.summary: Fail-loud loader for the Run Asset Index from a run directory.

nodes.name: write_rai
nodes.summary: Writes the canonical RAI JSON artifact (orchestrator-side writer, separated from loaders).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry for the Marcus orchestrator (4A conversation loop, plan-lock commit, downstream fan-out): re-exports routing and supervisor surfaces and defines the NegotiatorSeam; external callers use the Marcus facade instead of importing this package directly.

nodes.name: NegotiatorSeam
nodes.summary: Seam object exposing the negotiation surface of the 4A conversation loop to the supervisor.

nodes.name: chooser_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Publishes the per-slide Storyboard-A A/B chooser page to GitHub Pages at gate G2B: renders the chooser HTML from Gary's per-slide PNGs and pushes a self-contained pack so the operator can pick variants slide-by-slide; the selection code returns as the G2B verdict.

nodes.name: _per_slide_options
nodes.summary: Collects the A/B variant options (per-slide PNGs and metadata) from Gary's dispatched outputs.

nodes.name: publish_chooser_for_gate
nodes.summary: Renders the per-slide A/B chooser HTML pack and publishes it to gh-pages for the G2B pick.

nodes.name: _git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: Delegates the clone-stage-commit-push transport to the shared gh-pages publish helper.

nodes.name: conversation_persistence.py
nodes.summary: Persists Marcus conversation turns with a tamper-evident SHA256 hash chain: canonical JSON digesting, chained turn writes, ordered loading, and full chain verification that fails loud on breaks.

nodes.name: canonical_json_bytes
nodes.summary: Serializes a payload to canonical sorted JSON bytes for stable digesting.

nodes.name: compute_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA256 chain digest of a turn over its canonical bytes plus the previous turn digest.

nodes.name: latest_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Returns the digest of the most recent persisted turn for chain continuation.

nodes.name: load_turn

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the LiteLLM Batch transport (Marcus-SPOC), re-exporting the adapter, error types, join utilities, JSONL encoding, and receipt helpers. Its docstring pins the binding rule that the product batch path is the provider Batch API, never litellm.batch_completion.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel with explicit __all__ and a governance docstring forbidding the litellm.batch_completion parallel-sync helper as the batch path.

nodes.name: adapter.py
nodes.summary: Thin LiteLLM Files + Batches SDK adapter that uploads JSONL, creates/retrieves/cancels provider batches, downloads outputs, and normalizes results into BatchReceipt and join types. Injectable SDK callables enable hermetic testing, and a source guard refuses litellm.batch_completion as the transport.
nodes.languageNotes: Constructor validates injected callables at runtime (AST/source-guarded in hermetic tests) so the forbidden batch_completion path can never be wired in.

nodes.name: cost_report.py
nodes.summary: Builds and persists per-run batch cost reports by aggregating token usage from joined output rows, pricing them via the cascade pricing table, and comparing against realtime cost. Includes a fail-soft emitter so cost reporting never blocks the production walk.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Error types for the LLM batch transport: LlmBatchError (tagged base error) and WaitingForProviderBatchError, the control-flow signal that a submitted provider batch is still pending so the orchestrator can pause and resume later.

nodes.name: join.py
nodes.summary: Joins provider batch output JSONL rows back to their custom_ids, classifying each row as ok/failed and reporting missing or unexpected ids against the expected set. Provides parse_output_jsonl for decoding raw output files.

nodes.name: jsonl.py
nodes.summary: Encodes batch request rows to JSONL with a byte-size budget guard, and builds /v1/chat/completions request rows (custom_id, model, messages, optional extra_body) for provider Batch submission.

nodes.name: prompt_cache.py
nodes.summary: Derives provider prompt-cache keys from configured strategies, resolves the vision node's cache key from the LLM execution config, builds the extra_body payload carrying the key, and extracts cached-token counts from usage objects.

nodes.name: receipts.py
nodes.summary: Defines the BatchReceipt model (batch id, file ids, status, counts, timestamps) and its read/write persistence under the run directory, plus normalization of heterogeneous LiteLLM batch objects into receipts. Receipts are the durable resume anchor for pending provider batches.

nodes.name: llm_batch_eligibility.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models and loader for the batch-eligibility matrix (A3): per-site flags declaring which pipeline sites are batch-eligible and v1-routed, with validators enforcing that vision is present and consistently marked. Gates whether the vision batch route may run at all.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 model validators enforce cross-field invariants (v1_routed implies batch_eligible; vision site must exist and be v1-routable).

nodes.name: llm_execution_config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models and loader for the per-node LLM execution config: each node declares realtime and batch transport profiles (provider, model, token limits, prompt-cache strategy) plus a default mode, resolved via resolve_profile. Vision-node presence is validated at load time.

nodes.name: batch_route.py
nodes.summary: Vision Batch route (B2): builds multimodal perception JSONL rows for slides, submits them through the LiteLLM Batch adapter, persists receipts, honors pause-vs-poll wait policies, then joins outputs back per slide and parses them into VisionProviderResponse results with a fail-soft cost report. Active only when llm_execution_mode is exactly 'batch' and the eligibility matrix marks vision v1-routable.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses WaitingForProviderBatchError as a resumable control-flow exception under the 'pause' wait policy, letting the orchestrator checkpoint and resume once the provider batch completes.

nodes.name: _coerce_file_bytes
nodes.summary: Coerces heterogeneous LiteLLM file_content return shapes (bytes, str, response objects with .content or .read(), text attributes) into raw bytes, raising a tagged LlmBatchError on unsupported types.

nodes.name: LiteLlmBatchAdapter
nodes.summary: Thin LiteLLM Batch client with injectable SDK callables (create_file, create_batch, retrieve, cancel, file_content) for hermetic testing. Offers upload_batch_jsonl, batch lifecycle methods, submit_and_receipt (submit plus persisted BatchReceipt), and join_completed_output which downloads output/error files and joins rows by custom_id.

nodes.name: _usage_from_row
nodes.summary: Safely digs the usage block (prompt/completion/cached token counts) out of a joined batch row's raw response body, returning None when any layer is missing or malformed.

nodes.name: build_batch_cost_report
nodes.summary: Aggregates token usage across joined batch rows, prices it against the cascade pricing table (with batch-discount vs realtime comparison), computes turnaround time from receipt timestamps, and assembles the full cost-report dict including ok/failed/missing row counts and diagnostic notes.

nodes.name: write_batch_cost_report
nodes.summary: Persists a batch cost report as JSON under the run's llm_batch directory, creating parent directories as needed, and returns the written path.

nodes.name: emit_batch_cost_report_fail_soft
nodes.summary: Fail-soft wrapper that builds and writes the batch cost report but swallows any exception, returning None so cost reporting can never break the production run.

nodes.name: LlmBatchError
nodes.summary: Base exception for the LLM batch transport carrying an optional machine-readable tag for classification of failure sites.

nodes.name: WaitingForProviderBatchError
nodes.summary: Control-flow exception raised when a provider batch has been submitted but is not yet terminal; carries run_id, batch_id, status, and receipt path so the orchestrator can pause the walk and resume once the batch completes.

nodes.name: JoinedBatchRow
nodes.summary: Immutable record of one joined batch output row: custom_id, ok flag, response/error payloads, and the raw row dict.

nodes.name: JoinResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate join outcome mapping custom_id to JoinedBatchRow, preserving order seen and listing missing/unexpected ids, with ok_ids and failed_ids convenience accessors.

nodes.name: _row_ok
nodes.summary: Classifies a raw output row as successful: no error field, a dict response, and an HTTP 2xx status code.

nodes.name: join_output_rows
nodes.summary: Joins raw output rows to expected custom_ids, rejecting rows without ids and duplicate ids, classifying each row ok/failed, and computing missing and unexpected id sets into a JoinResult.

nodes.name: parse_output_jsonl
nodes.summary: Decodes provider output JSONL (bytes or str) into a list of row dicts, skipping blank lines and non-dict entries.

nodes.name: estimate_jsonl_bytes
nodes.summary: Estimates the encoded JSONL byte size of a row sequence using compact JSON separators plus newline overhead.

nodes.name: assert_within_size_budget
nodes.summary: Raises a tagged LlmBatchError when the estimated JSONL payload exceeds the provider upload byte budget, reporting actual vs allowed size.

nodes.name: encode_batch_jsonl
nodes.summary: Encodes request rows into a newline-terminated JSONL byte payload after asserting the size budget.

nodes.name: make_chat_completions_row
nodes.summary: Builds a single /v1/chat/completions batch request row with custom_id, model, messages, optional max_completion_tokens/temperature, and merged extra_body (e.g., prompt-cache key).

nodes.name: derive_prompt_cache_key
nodes.summary: Maps a configured prompt-cache-key strategy name to a concrete cache key for a node, falling back to treating unknown strategies as literal keys.

nodes.name: resolve_vision_prompt_cache_key
nodes.summary: Loads the LLM execution config, resolves the vision node's transport profile for the given mode, and derives the prompt-cache key from its configured strategy, validating mode values.

nodes.name: prompt_cache_extra_body
nodes.summary: Wraps a resolved cache key into the extra_body dict shape ({'prompt_cache_key': ...}) expected by chat-completions request rows, returning None when no key applies.

nodes.name: extract_cached_tokens
nodes.summary: Extracts the cached_tokens count from a usage object's prompt_tokens_details, defensively handling missing or malformed layers by returning 0.

nodes.name: BatchReceipt
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of a durable batch submission receipt: run/batch/file ids, status, endpoint, provider, model, completion window, timestamps, row and request counts, and metadata. Serves as the resume anchor for pending provider batches.

nodes.name: write_receipt
nodes.summary: Serializes a BatchReceipt to JSON at the canonical receipt path under the run directory, creating directories as needed.

nodes.name: read_receipt
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the persisted BatchReceipt for a run from its canonical receipt path, returning None if absent.

nodes.name: normalize_batch_object
nodes.summary: Normalizes heterogeneous LiteLLM batch return objects (dicts or attribute objects) into a BatchReceipt, coercing ids, statuses, timestamps, request counts, and metadata with defensive fallbacks.

nodes.name: EligibilitySite
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of one pipeline site's batch eligibility: batch_eligible and v1_routed flags, criteria met, and rationale, with a validator enforcing that v1-routing implies eligibility.

nodes.name: BatchEligibilityMatrix
nodes.summary: Top-level eligibility matrix mapping site names to EligibilitySite entries, validating that the vision site exists and is v1-routable, with query helpers (site, is_batch_eligible, is_v1_batch_routable, v1_routable_sites).

nodes.name: load_batch_eligibility
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the batch-eligibility matrix YAML from its default state/config path (or an override) into a BatchEligibilityMatrix.

nodes.name: NodeTransportProfile
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of a single transport profile for a node: provider, model, max completion tokens, reasoning effort, verbosity, prompt-cache strategy/retention, batch model fallback family, and harness baseline batch id.

nodes.name: NodeExecutionProfile
nodes.summary: Per-node execution profile holding default_mode plus realtime and batch NodeTransportProfiles, with profile_for(mode) selecting the transport for a requested mode and rejecting unknown modes.

nodes.name: LlmExecutionConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level LLM execution config mapping node names to NodeExecutionProfiles with a global default mode; validates that the vision node exists and exposes node() and resolve_profile() lookups.

nodes.name: load_llm_execution
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the LLM execution YAML config from its default state/config path (or an override) into an LlmExecutionConfig.

nodes.name: resolve_vision_execution_mode
nodes.summary: Reads llm_execution_mode from the specialist payload, defaulting to 'realtime'; only the exact string 'batch' turns the batch route ON.

nodes.name: _assert_unique_slide_ids
nodes.summary: Validates that every slide has a non-empty, unique slide_id, raising VisionProviderError on blanks or duplicates so custom_ids remain unambiguous.

nodes.name: build_vision_batch_rows
nodes.summary: Builds one chat-completions batch row per slide: resolves the vision prompt-cache key, constructs multimodal perception messages for each PNG, and packages them with per-slide custom_ids for provider Batch submission.

nodes.name: extract_assistant_text
nodes.summary: Digs the assistant message text out of a joined batch row's raw response (response body, choices, message, content), raising VisionProviderError with the custom_id when any layer is malformed or empty.

nodes.name: poll_until_terminal
nodes.summary: Polls the adapter for the batch's status at a fixed interval until it reaches a terminal state or a timeout elapses, normalizing each polled object into a BatchReceipt and raising a tagged LlmBatchError on timeout.

nodes.name: run_vision_batch_perception
nodes.summary: End-to-end vision batch perception: checks eligibility and execution config, resumes from a persisted receipt or builds rows and submits a new batch, honors 'pause' (raise WaitingForProviderBatchError) vs 'poll' wait policies, then joins completed output, emits a fail-soft cost report, and parses each slide's assistant text into VisionProviderResponse results in input order.

nodes.name: selection_policy_validators.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder module reserving the four-file-lockstep validator slot for ModelSelectionPolicy cross-field checks; the no-silent-conflicts invariant currently lives inline in the model itself pending future selector enhancements.

nodes.name: _flake_rate.py
nodes.summary: Per-cell flake-rate calculator for TW-7c-6 scaffold checks: Pydantic input/verdict models plus an evaluator that checks whether a test cell's failure rate stays within its class-specific budget (pre-7c 0.1% vs 7c-added 0.05%).
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 models with extra='forbid', validate_assignment=True, and an after-mode model_validator enforcing failed_runs <= total_runs.

nodes.name: CellFlakeInput
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a per-cell run result (cell id, class, total/failed runs) with a validator rejecting failed_runs above total_runs.

nodes.name: CellFlakeVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic verdict model carrying the computed flake rate, applicable budget, and within_budget boolean for a cell.

nodes.name: budget_for_cell_class
nodes.summary: Returns the flake budget constant for a given cell class (pre-7c or 7c-added).

nodes.name: evaluate_cell_flake_budget
nodes.summary: Computes a cell's flake rate from its run counts and returns a CellFlakeVerdict comparing it against the class budget.

nodes.name: tw_7c_3_firing.py
nodes.summary: Single-source TW-7c-3 four-file-lockstep firing rule: given a decision-card gate id, verifies that all four required files (model, JSON schema, shape-pin test, golden fixture) exist in the repo and reports any missing kinds.

nodes.name: LockstepResult
nodes.summary: Pydantic result model for the lockstep check: gate id, per-kind file presence map, all_four_present flag, and optional failure reason.

nodes.name: LOCKSTEP_CHECK
nodes.summary: Resolves the four expected file paths for a gate id from FOUR_FILE_GLOBS templates, checks each for existence, and returns a LockstepResult naming any missing kinds.

nodes.name: parity_comparison.py
nodes.summary: Head-to-head parity comparison tool for Slab 5a.2: canonicalizes control-plane artifacts (course/module context, asset specs, motion plan) from a primary bundle and a clone run, scores tier-1 presence and tier-2 semantic match against thresholds, and renders a markdown evidence report via a CLI entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses regex-based canonicalization (run-id, UUID, timestamp scrubbing) so semantically equal artifacts compare equal despite run-specific identifiers; artifact pairs are declared as data via ArtifactParitySpec with canonicalizer callables.

nodes.name: ArtifactParitySpec
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass declaring one comparable artifact: label, description, primary/clone relative paths, and the canonicalizer callables for each side.

nodes.name: ArtifactParityResult
nodes.summary: Per-artifact comparison outcome (presence flags, structural match score, matched/comparable line counts, divergence rationale) with tier1_present and tier2_passes helper properties.

nodes.name: ParityComparisonReport
nodes.summary: Aggregate parity report: roots and baseline paths, comparison timestamp, tier-1/tier-2 scores against thresholds, artifact results, and notes.

nodes.name: _normalize_scalar
nodes.summary: Normalizes a scalar value for comparison: collapses whitespace, strips path prefixes, and replaces run ids, UUIDs, and timestamps with stable placeholders before lowercasing.

nodes.name: _flatten_mapping
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a nested mapping (dicts and lists) into sorted key-path=value lines with normalized scalars, producing a canonical line set for semantic diffing.

nodes.name: _compare_semantic_lines
nodes.summary: Compares the flattened canonical line sets of primary vs clone mappings, computing a structural match score, matched/comparable counts, and a divergence rationale listing mismatched lines.

nodes.name: compare_actual_substrate_parity
nodes.summary: Walks every ArtifactParitySpec, canonicalizes both sides, scores semantic match per artifact, aggregates tier-1 (presence) and tier-2 (match) scores, and returns a ParityComparisonReport with notes on missing baseline or artifacts.

nodes.name: render_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a ParityComparisonReport as a markdown evidence document with summary tables, per-artifact sections, and pass/fail tier verdicts.

nodes.name: write_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered parity evidence markdown for a report to the given output path.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Argparse CLI entry point: parses bundle/run/baseline/output paths, runs compare_actual_substrate_parity, writes the evidence markdown, and prints a summary.

nodes.summary: Brief package readme identifying app.runtime as the Slab 1 runtime substrate package and citing its justifying functional requirements (FR1, FR2, FR3, FR7).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer marking app.runtime as the Slab 1 runtime substrate package scaffold; docstring only.

nodes.name: checkpointer.py
nodes.summary: AsyncPostgresSaver factory for LangGraph checkpoint persistence: resolves DATABASE_URL from the environment, normalizes it via psycopg conninfo, and yields a ready checkpointer as an async context manager with idempotent schema setup.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TYPE_CHECKING-guarded imports and an asynccontextmanager wrapper matching AsyncPostgresSaver.from_conn_string's native API shape.

nodes.name: CheckpointerConfigError
nodes.summary: Named exception raised when checkpointer configuration (DATABASE_URL) is missing or invalid.

nodes.name: resolve_database_url
nodes.summary: Reads DATABASE_URL from the environment, raising CheckpointerConfigError with a named error surface when unset.

nodes.name: resolve_checkpointer_conninfo
nodes.summary: Normalizes the resolved database URL into a psycopg conninfo string via make_conninfo.

nodes.name: make_checkpointer
nodes.summary: Async context manager factory yielding an AsyncPostgresSaver built from the resolved conninfo, running setup() so the checkpoints table schema exists.

nodes.name: cleanup_threads.py
nodes.summary: Operator-invoked CLI that deletes aged LangGraph checkpoint threads from Postgres per the RetentionPolicy (max thread age, retain-completed/failed flags), reading status and timestamp tags from checkpoint metadata; supports dry-run and apply modes and never self-schedules.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents a metadata contract (metadata->>'status', updated_at/created_at) the cleanup SQL consumes; untagged checkpoints are retained by design.

nodes.name: CleanupResult
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing a cleanup run outcome: deleted thread count, dry-run flag, and policy version.

nodes.name: _threads_to_delete
nodes.summary: Filters candidate thread rows against the retention policy's retain_completed and retain_failed flags, returning thread ids eligible for deletion.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.summary: Queries the checkpoints table for threads older than the policy's max age, applies retain-flag filtering, and either reports (dry-run) or deletes the eligible threads in a single committed transaction.

nodes.name: _build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse parser with a mutually exclusive --dry-run/--apply mode group and a policy path option.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: loads the retention policy, prints its summary, connects to Postgres via DATABASE_URL, runs cleanup, and returns 0 on success or 1 on named failure surfaces.

nodes.name: retention_policy.py
nodes.summary: Operator-facing checkpoint retention configuration: Pydantic RetentionPolicy model (max thread age, documentation-only cron hint, retain-completed/failed flags) plus a strict YAML loader; deliberately lives under app.runtime rather than graph state.
nodes.languageNotes: cron_hint is validated with a permissive five-field regex on purpose — it is documentation for an externally run cron, not a parsed schedule.

nodes.name: RetentionPolicy
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for checkpoint retention config (max_thread_age_days, cleanup_cron_hint, retain_completed, retain_failed) with extra='forbid' and a field validator enforcing five-field cron shape.

nodes.name: load_policy
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a RetentionPolicy from a YAML file (default state/config/retention-policy.yaml), failing loudly on missing files or non-mapping content.

nodes.name: retry_policy.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-aware retry helpers for LangGraph nodes: translates ValidationError from node-boundary model_validate calls into a runtime-owned retryable exception and builds a matching RetryPolicy so transiently invalid provider payloads trigger node retries instead of failing the graph.
nodes.languageNotes: Works around LangGraph's default RetryPolicy not treating raw Pydantic ValidationError as retryable; pairs validate_for_retry with pydantic_retry_policy at node registration.

nodes.name: RetryableValidationNodeError
nodes.summary: Runtime-owned retryable exception wrapping a Pydantic ValidationError with the originating node name so LangGraph retry policies can target it.

nodes.name: validate_for_retry
nodes.summary: Validates a payload against a Pydantic model type, re-raising ValidationError as RetryableValidationNodeError tagged with the node name.

nodes.name: pydantic_retry_policy
nodes.summary: Builds a LangGraph RetryPolicy (interval, backoff, max attempts, jitter) that retries on RetryableValidationNodeError.

nodes.name: override_warning.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema (v1) pinning the ModelOverrideWarning payload shape for operator-visible model-override warnings emitted by the runtime; additive-safe versioned contract.

nodes.name: sanctum_warning.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema (v1) pinning the SanctumWarning payload shape — a single operator-visible sanctum mutation warning — as a versioned runtime contract.

nodes.name: span_tags.py
nodes.summary: LangSmith span-tag contract pin exporting the REQUIRED_SPAN_TAG_KEYS frozenset (trial_id, node_id, agent, lane) that every runtime span must carry; changing the set is a governed downstream-breaking schema change.

nodes.summary: Builds an importlib spec from a file path, executes the module, and returns it, raising ImportError when the spec cannot be created.

nodes.name: research_intake.py
nodes.summary: Research retrieval intake for Agentic Research Foundations R6: converts wrangled research entries (R4 credibility + R3 triangulation) into provenance items and attribution phrases, guards against fabricated citations, and assembles a durable ResearchIntakePacket for Irene Pass-2 and narration.
nodes.languageNotes: Lives under app.specialists._shared deliberately so both Marcus wiring and Irene can import it — M3 forbids specialists importing app.marcus.orchestrator.

nodes.name: FabricatedCitationError
nodes.summary: Named exception raised when cited source ids do not resolve to any wrangled research entry, closing the fabricate-cite hole.

nodes.name: RetrievalProvenanceItem
nodes.summary: Pydantic model recording one source's retrieval provenance: source id, providers, citation id, evidence hierarchy tier, peer-review flag, triangulation status, reliability score, and attribution phrase.

nodes.name: ResearchIntakePacket
nodes.summary: Pydantic packet summarizing a research intake for a cluster: intake mode, evidence-bolster flag, entries consumed, retrieval provenance list, known losses, and deduplicated attribution phrases.

nodes.name: attribution_phrase_for_entry
nodes.summary: Selects the convergence narration pattern (dual-provider, scite-only, consensus-only, etc.) for a research entry based on its providers and triangulation status.

nodes.name: build_retrieval_provenance
nodes.summary: Maps wrangled research entries into RetrievalProvenanceItem records, skipping malformed entries and attaching computed attribution phrases.

nodes.name: resolvable_source_ids
nodes.summary: Collects the set of citable identifiers (source ids in both original and lowercase form, plus source refs) that legitimately resolve to wrangled entries.

nodes.name: assert_no_fabricated_citations
nodes.summary: Verifies every cited source id resolves against the wrangled entries, raising FabricatedCitationError listing any unresolvable citations.

nodes.name: consume_research_entries
nodes.summary: Top-level intake: builds provenance from entries, records known losses for malformed input, deduplicates attribution phrases, and returns a ResearchIntakePacket for the cluster with the given intake mode and evidence-bolster flag.

nodes.name: composition.py
nodes.summary: Component composition engine that selects and orders manifest fragments, bridges edges around excluded components, prunes absent optional inputs, and produces a deterministic composed manifest with a replayable content digest.

nodes.name: compose_manifest
nodes.summary: Composes a manifest from a component selection, resolving order, bridging edges around excluded nodes, and pruning absent optional inputs.

nodes.name: compose_and_digest
nodes.summary: Composes a manifest and computes a replayable content digest over the selection, manifest, and dispatch snapshot.

nodes.name: ComponentFragment
nodes.summary: Describes a composable manifest fragment: its component, modality refs, owned node ids, dependencies, synthetic nodes, and attachment point.

nodes.name: operator_surface_assembler.py
nodes.summary: Assembles and atomically persists the operator-surface projection during a production run, merging step, health, trace, and decision-card updates emitted by the orchestrator.

nodes.name: OperatorSurfaceAssembler
nodes.summary: Stateful assembler that merges incremental orchestrator updates into the operator-surface projection and atomically writes it for the HUD to consume.

nodes.name: PreflightResult
nodes.summary: Result of a preflight run holding all check items and exposing blocking-item selection.

nodes.name: .gitattributes
nodes.summary: Git attribute rules controlling line-ending normalization and diff/merge handling for repository paths.

nodes.name: workbook_writer_model_config.yaml
nodes.summary: Model-resolution config for the workbook-writer specialist: specialist id, default model, per-node overrides, and default temperature.

nodes.name: 07W-companion-workbook-producer.md.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 template rendering the section 07W companion-workbook producer step that generates the lesson workbook.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Argparse entry point for `python -m app.marcus.cli`, wiring the trial, gate, adhoc, plan-dialogue, and plan-ratify subcommands into one dispatcher.

nodes.name: plan_dialogue_cli.py
nodes.summary: Interactive pre-start planning REPL (Mine 2A) that elicits purpose/audience/workflow/gap-fill/LOs from the operator, writes ratification companions plus ratified-los.json with confirm-before-write, and persists a transcript.

nodes.name: plan_ratify_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI adapter that assesses source material, ratifies a planning decision, and writes ratification artifacts — a thin shell over source_assessment and planning_ratification.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the course-source public API: asset records, canonical processed-source validators, input bundles, manifest scan/drift, models, registry, SME registry, and syllabus metadata.

nodes.name: asset_records.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for canonical course asset records (readings, lectures, labs, assessments) with source refs and review status, plus factories for syllabus-requirement and lesson-LO records and requirement-gap emission.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 models with extra='forbid' and model_validator invariants per the project schema checklist.

nodes.name: canonical_processed_source.py
nodes.summary: Contract and validators for the canonical processed-source layout (Mine 4A): lesson leaves (slides/references/assessments), run dirs (bundle/extracted.md, g0-enrichment.json), and AssetKind shape-pins on enrichment nodes.

nodes.name: input_bundle.py
nodes.summary: Builds the LessonPlanningInputBundle: course/module planning profiles, scoped source manifest, styleguide resolution, and asset records assembled from course scans and syllabus proposals.

nodes.name: manifest_drift.py
nodes.summary: Detects drift between a stored course-source manifest snapshot and a fresh scan via reproducible YAML diffing.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Assembles the top-level argparse parser and registers all Marcus CLI subcommands (trial, gate, adhoc, plan-dialogue, plan-ratify).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Dispatches the parsed subcommand to its handler and returns the process exit code.

nodes.name: PlanDialogueSession
nodes.summary: Stateful REPL session that runs the elicitation turns, confirms before writing ratification artifacts and ratified LOs, and persists the dialogue transcript.

nodes.name: build_plan_dialogue_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse subparser for the plan-dialogue command, including scripted-input and run-dir options.

nodes.name: _load_script
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a scripted dialogue-answer file used to drive the REPL non-interactively.

nodes.name: run_plan_dialogue
nodes.summary: Constructs a PlanDialogueSession from parsed args and executes the full dialogue-to-artifacts flow.

nodes.name: plan_dialogue_cli
nodes.summary: Argument-parsing wrapper that runs the plan dialogue and maps errors to exit codes.

nodes.name: build_plan_ratify_parser
nodes.summary: Defines the argparse surface for the plan-ratify command (purpose, audience, workflow, gap-fill, collateral, and output options).

nodes.name: _assess
nodes.summary: Chooses between input-bundle and corpus-dir source assessment based on the CLI arguments.

nodes.name: run_plan_ratify
nodes.summary: Runs assess, ratify, and write-artifacts for one planning decision and prints the resulting record.

nodes.name: plan_ratify_cli
nodes.summary: Argument-parsing wrapper mapping ratification errors to exit codes.

nodes.name: CanonicalAssetRecord
nodes.summary: Core asset-record model with kind, status, and source refs, plus a validator forcing source-grounded records to carry a content ref.

nodes.name: make_syllabus_requirement_record
nodes.summary: Factory building a canonical asset record for a syllabus-declared requirement.

nodes.name: make_lesson_lo_record
nodes.summary: Factory building a canonical asset record for a lesson learning objective.

nodes.name: emit_requirement_gap_records
nodes.summary: Derives gap asset-records for syllabus requirements that no scanned source satisfies.

nodes.name: CanonicalValidationResult
nodes.summary: Aggregates per-path validation errors and digests with raise_if_failed and dict serialization.

nodes.name: validate_lesson_leaf
nodes.summary: Validates a lesson leaf directory against the canonical slides/references/assessments layout.

nodes.name: validate_run_dir
nodes.summary: Validates a run directory's canonical artifacts, including the enrichment AssetKind shape-pin.

nodes.name: validate_canonical_tree
nodes.summary: Walks a course tree validating every lesson leaf and run directory.

nodes.name: annotate_typed_components_with_kind
nodes.summary: Annotates typed enrichment components with their resolved AssetKind for the Mine-5 shape-pin.

nodes.name: write_canonical_contract_doc
nodes.summary: Renders the canonical-layout contract documentation file to disk.

nodes.name: LessonPlanningInputBundle
nodes.summary: Top-level bundle model tying planning profiles, scoped manifest, styleguide resolution, and asset records, with a scope/gap consistency validator.

nodes.name: _scoped_manifest
nodes.summary: Filters the full course manifest down to the entries in scope for one module or lesson.

nodes.name: build_lesson_planning_input_bundle
nodes.summary: Assembles the full input bundle for a lesson by scanning the course, resolving module profiles and styleguide, and emitting gap records.

nodes.name: check_manifest_snapshot
nodes.summary: Compares a persisted manifest snapshot against a fresh scan and returns a diff-bearing drift result.

nodes.name: manifest_scan.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic git-aware scanner that inventories course-source files into a SourceManifest and derives a gap ledger (empty dirs, unsupported formats, module mismatches, availability drift).

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic record types for course-source ingestion planning: course/module records, SME and source-availability records, source-manifest entries, and the gap ledger.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a course.yaml container declaration into a CourseRecord.

nodes.name: syllabus_metadata.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic syllabus readers (DOCX and MHTML) that extract anchored course/module metadata and objectives, and build module-metadata proposals with mojibake and format-unsupported gap detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stdlib email/html parsers for MHTML plus python-docx; every extracted value carries a SourceAnchor for provenance.

nodes.name: collateral_spec.py
nodes.summary: CollateralSpec content-model family (Braid S1/DP4): workbook sections bound to learning objectives, depth-delta contracts, Bloom-leveled exercises with source-grounded answer keys, and pedagogically-framed research-enrichment goals.

nodes.name: coverage_annotation.py
nodes.summary: Authored side of the coverage interlock (T4): one CoverageAnnotation per note-bearing component carrying re-segmented SourcePoint assertions, with deterministic risk flags, verbatim-span re-anchoring, segmentation-completeness asserts, and an offline pass plus a live gpt-5 segmentation leg.
nodes.languageNotes: Live LLM leg runs with hard per-request timeouts and max_retries=0; identity keys on verbatim spans so the model paraphrase is never trusted.

nodes.name: coverage_gate.py
nodes.summary: Neutral fail-loud coverage gate logic (T6): blocks the pipeline before audio spend only on deterministically-certain missing must-cover points, keeps fuzzy verdicts warn-only, and raises CoverageAssuranceError through the existing SpecialistDispatchError channel.

nodes.name: coverage_receipt.py
nodes.summary: Derived two-axis coverage ledger (T5/T7): joins source points to deck/narration anchors (Axis A coverage) and applies Vera-R7 containment verdicts (Axis B) with vouch-level honesty fuses, canonical hashing, and plan-view projections.

nodes.name: _file_entries
nodes.summary: Builds manifest entries for every file under the course root with scope, role, module/lesson ids, git status, and content hash.

nodes.name: _gaps
nodes.summary: Derives the gap ledger: empty source/lesson dirs, unsupported formats, module count mismatches, and declared-vs-detected drift.

nodes.name: scan_course
nodes.summary: Entry point producing the CourseSourceScan (source manifest plus gap ledger) for a course root.

nodes.name: CourseRecord
nodes.summary: Top-level course declaration with a schema-version pin, course details, runtime contract, modules, and source availability.

nodes.name: load_course
nodes.summary: Reads course.yaml under a course root and validates it into a CourseRecord.

nodes.name: ModuleMetadataProposal
nodes.summary: Proposal model for one module's metadata whose proposed records must carry source anchors.

nodes.name: _HtmlTableParser
nodes.summary: Minimal HTMLParser subclass that captures table rows and cells from MHTML syllabus content.

nodes.name: read_docx_syllabus
nodes.summary: Extracts anchored text and tables from a DOCX syllabus into an ExtractedSyllabusDocument.

nodes.name: read_mhtml_syllabus
nodes.summary: Parses an MHTML-saved syllabus page into an anchored extracted document.

nodes.name: build_module_metadata_proposal
nodes.summary: Builds a course metadata proposal from a syllabus file path by dispatching to the right format reader.

nodes.name: build_module_metadata_proposal_from_document
nodes.summary: Derives course title, learner profile, objectives, and per-module proposals from an extracted syllabus document.

nodes.name: DepthDeltaContract
nodes.summary: Declares what depth is deferred off the glance slide into the workbook, with open-id slide refs and non-empty deferred-depth validation.

nodes.name: Exercise
nodes.summary: Workbook exercise with a closed Bloom level and a source-grounded answer_key_source_ref slot, both shape-validated.

nodes.name: WorkbookSection
nodes.summary: Workbook section binding a mandatory learning_objective_id and a depth-delta contract.

nodes.name: WorkbookSpec
nodes.summary: Container spec listing the workbook's sections — the single source of truth the workbook producer builds from.

nodes.name: ResearchEnrichmentGoal
nodes.summary: Research goal expressed as pedagogical intent (never a raw fetch query), validated against raw-query smells.

nodes.name: CollateralSpec
nodes.summary: Discriminated top-level collateral spec whose kind discriminant must match the carried payload.

nodes.name: CoverageAnnotation
nodes.summary: Frozen annotation model per component enforcing block invariants and the v1-shippable check.

nodes.name: detect_risk_flags
nodes.summary: Deterministically flags risk-bearing spans (numerics, negation, comparators) in a source point.

nodes.name: assert_v1_shippable
nodes.summary: Fail-loud assert that every annotation meets the v1 shippable contract.

nodes.name: detect_incomplete_segmentation
nodes.summary: Finds note-block text not covered by any assertion span (incomplete segmentation).

nodes.name: reanchor_verbatim_span
nodes.summary: Re-anchors an LLM-cut span to the exact verbatim source text, straightening quotes and tolerating whitespace drift.

nodes.name: detect_non_verbatim_spans
nodes.summary: Reports spans that fail verbatim anchoring against the source block.

nodes.name: build_coverage_from_rows
nodes.summary: Builds CoverageAnnotation objects (with SourcePoints) from segmentation rows, assigning deterministic ids, risk flags, and coverage intents.

nodes.name: build_coverage_annotations
nodes.summary: Top-level pass producing coverage annotations offline by default or via the live gpt-5 segmentation leg.

nodes.name: CoverageAssuranceError
nodes.summary: SpecialistDispatchError subclass carrying the blocking receipt rows so the runner routes the halt through _pause_at_error.

nodes.name: _is_blocking
nodes.summary: Decides whether a receipt row is deterministically blocking (must-cover, missing or verbatim-absent, no planned surface).

nodes.name: coverage_warnings
nodes.summary: Collects warn-only ledger findings (altered, risky, low-confidence rows) from a receipt.

nodes.name: evaluate_vacuous_receipt
nodes.summary: Detects vacuous receipts (no joined rows or everything excluded) that would render the gate meaningless.

nodes.name: assert_coverage_gate
