nodes.summary: Generic base for live structured-output writers: loads the specialist model config, invokes the chat model with schema-constrained output, retries, and records execution metadata for receipts.

nodes.name: LiveSceneComposer
nodes.summary: Live LLM scene composer implementing the scene_extraction composer seam, constraining generation with the seed-derived faithfulness prompt constraints.

nodes.name: LivePromiseTransformer
nodes.summary: Live LLM transformer for the workbook promise beat, converting typed prework inputs into the promise projection payload.

nodes.name: _compact_deep_dive_payload
nodes.summary: Compacts the Deep Dive skeleton request into a token-efficient provider payload while preserving the claims and markers the gate later verifies.

nodes.name: _is_bold_parity_failure
nodes.summary: Detects whether a Deep Dive gate loss is specifically a prose/bold-term parity failure, the trigger for the safe-construction degradation path.

nodes.name: LiveDeepDiveWriter
nodes.summary: Live LLM Deep Dive skeleton writer: invokes the structured provider, validates against the deep-dive gates, and on bold-parity failure degrades to emitting a deterministic safe construction instead of failing the band.

nodes.name: workbook_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic orchestration seam for the four-node workbook band: resolves the slide authority map under a dispatch lock, composes the Deep Dive with journaled exactly-once provider dispatch, builds the workbook brief, wires Ask-A/Ask-B/review stubs, and reconciles activated artifacts into the production envelope.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses OS-level file locks (msvcrt on Windows, fcntl elsewhere), atomic JSON writes with directory fsync, and journal recovery for crash-safe exactly-once node execution.

nodes.name: _slide_authority_dispatch_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager acquiring the per-run slide-authority dispatch lock via OS file locking so only one process resolves and persists the authority map at a time.

nodes.name: _resolve_slide_authority
nodes.summary: Resolves the workbook slide authority map for the run: gathers manifest segments, plan units, and package slides from envelope contributions, builds the map through slide_authority, and persists or validates it idempotently.

nodes.name: _provider_evidence
nodes.summary: Extracts and normalizes provider execution evidence (model identity, payload digests, timing) from a writer invocation for inclusion in the Deep Dive execution receipt.

nodes.name: _compose_deep_dive
nodes.summary: Composes the 07W.1 Deep Dive: builds the skeleton request from prework authority, dispatches the (live or offline) writer with journaled exactly-once semantics, persists provider failures, and produces the digest-bound execution receipt.

nodes.name: _brief_factory_locked
nodes.summary: Locked workbook-brief factory body: assembles the WorkbookBriefPayload from scene, promise, deep-dive, and enrichment inputs, writes the brief artifact atomically, and returns its contribution receipt.

nodes.name: runtime_context_for_run
nodes.summary: Builds the WorkbookBriefRuntimeContext for a run directory, validating the course source root and threading the identities downstream writers depend on.

nodes.name: _reconcile_activated_artifact
nodes.summary: Reconciles a previously activated workbook artifact against the current run state, replacing stale brief contributions or accepting the existing artifact when digests match.

nodes.name: run_workbook_band_node
nodes.summary: Main entry point executing one node of the four-node workbook band (07W.1-07W.4): dispatches to the correct factory (brief, Ask-A, review stub, Ask-B stub), resolves slide authority, and lands results as envelope contributions with full receipts.

nodes.name: pass1_source_section.py
nodes.summary: Dual-identity authenticated source sections for Irene Pass-1: pairs a raw-source identity with the authenticated extracted body and provides the canonical content digest under the Texas extraction contract.

nodes.name: canonical_extracted_content_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the sha256 digest of an extracted section body after newline normalization and trimming, per the Texas extraction contract.

nodes.name: Pass1AuthenticatedSourceSection
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass pairing one raw-source identity with one authenticated extracted body for Pass-1 consumption.

nodes.name: pass1_generation_lock.py
nodes.summary: Shared same-host lock guarding Pass-1 generation publication and consumption, so plan writers and downstream builders never interleave on the same run's artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Cross-platform OS file lock: msvcrt.locking on Windows, fcntl.flock elsewhere, wrapped in a contextmanager.

nodes.name: pass1_generation_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager acquiring the per-run Pass-1 generation file lock, raising Pass1GenerationLockError when the lock is unavailable or unsafe.

nodes.name: source_fidelity_audit.py
nodes.summary: L2 source-provenance audit measuring how faithfully a delivered narration's numeric assertions trace to the source corpus, plus a warn-only semantic-framing tripwire. Binding contract: it only ever returns a report and never gates or blocks production.
nodes.languageNotes: The only FAIL condition is a zero-denominator guard — when narration or source yields zero numeric tokens the audit cannot be performed.

nodes.name: audit_semantic_framing
nodes.summary: Warn-only heuristic tripwire reporting candidate framing sentences whose semantic claims may not be corpus-grounded; explicitly not a comprehensive claim-to-source faithfulness check.

nodes.name: audit_numeric_provenance
nodes.summary: Core numeric-provenance audit: tokenizes narration and source numerics, classifies each narration figure as sourced, research-supplement, or drift, and returns a WARN-only report with drift rate and nearest-token evidence.

nodes.name: _nearest_source_token
nodes.summary: Finds the closest source numeric token to a drifting narration figure so the report can show what the narration value most plausibly diverged from.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Gary consumed-payload contract: declares the frozen set of envelope payload keys Gary's act body reads, locking the lesson-plan to slide-briefs to Gamma chain into one vocabulary enforced by the Ratchet-D manifest lockstep test.
nodes.languageNotes: A single frozenset constant (CONSUMED_PAYLOAD_KEYS) serves as the machine-checked vocabulary boundary between the S3 package builder and Gary.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Irene Pass-1 lesson-plan coauthoring implementation: assembles the corpus-grounded planning prompt, invokes the LLM with context-window and identity checks, normalizes clusters/collateral/fidelity from the response, and writes the lesson plan plus plan-authority receipts under the Pass-1 generation lock with exactly-once replay validation.
nodes.languageNotes: The act/_act_locked split mirrors the repo-wide lock-wrapper pattern; response decoding rejects duplicate JSON keys and invalid constants via custom hooks.

nodes.name: _validate_completed_replay
nodes.summary: Validates that a previously completed Pass-1 execution being replayed matches the current inputs (mode, digests, plan identity) before its output is reused, enforcing exactly-once semantics.

nodes.name: validate_pass1_plan_authority
nodes.summary: Validates the Pass-1 plan authority receipt against the lesson plan it authorizes, raising Pass1AuthorityError on any identity or digest mismatch.

nodes.name: assemble_pass1_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the full Pass-1 coauthoring prompt from the authenticated source corpus, planning context, sanctum references, and cluster/fidelity/collateral emission instructions, keeping the corpus as the only topic basis.

nodes.name: normalize_clusters
nodes.summary: Normalizes the LLM's raw cluster emissions into validated plan-unit clusters, enforcing unique ids, singleton handling, and coverage rules before the plan is accepted.

nodes.name: normalize_collateral
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw collateral emissions from the response into validated CollateralSpec entries attached to plan units.

nodes.name: normalize_fidelity
nodes.summary: Normalizes the response's source-fidelity annotations into canonical form for the plan's fidelity record.

nodes.name: write_lesson_plan
nodes.summary: Persists the normalized lesson plan to the run directory atomically, recording its content digest for downstream authority validation.

nodes.name: write_plan_authority_receipt
nodes.summary: Writes the plan-authority receipt binding the persisted lesson plan's digest to the run and schema version, the artifact downstream builders validate against.

nodes.name: _write_current_plan_artifacts_locked
nodes.summary: Locked body that publishes the current plan artifacts (lesson plan, authority receipt, collateral lines) as one consistent set with fsync-safe atomic writes.

nodes.name: confirm_plan_units
nodes.summary: Confirms ratified plan units against operator verdicts, raising ratification errors when units are confirmed in bulk or out of authority.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Public specialist entry point: acquires the Pass-1 generation lock and delegates to the locked act body, so plan generation and publication are serialized per run.

nodes.name: _act_locked
nodes.summary: Locked act body: enforces Pass-1 mode, decodes the envelope payload, resolves the source bundle, assembles and dispatches the prompt (or validates a completed replay), normalizes the response, applies the cluster floor, and publishes plan artifacts with learning events.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Irene Pass-1 consumed-payload contract: the frozen set of envelope payload keys the Pass-1 act actually reads (mode flags, run placement, upstream output, bundle reference, cluster floor, planning context), published as the Ratchet-D vocabulary boundary.
nodes.languageNotes: min_cluster_floor is documented as runner context consumed post-hoc and stripped from the LLM-visible prompt.

nodes.name: source_fidelity_report.py
nodes.summary: CLI that runs the warn-only L2 numeric provenance audit on a trial run: loads the delivered narration from the segment manifest and the slide-markdown source corpus, then writes and prints the audit report for human review without gating anything.

nodes.name: load_narration
nodes.summary: Loads the delivered narration text by joining segment narration_text fields from the run's segment-manifest-storyboard-b.yaml export.

nodes.name: load_source_corpus
nodes.summary: Loads and concatenates the slide markdown files that constitute the source corpus for the audit.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, loads narration and source corpus, runs audit_numeric_provenance, and writes the report into the run's exports directory.

nodes.name: quinn_r_gate.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic Quinn-R step-13 two-branch quality gate for lesson plan units: non-blueprint units pass via produced-asset quality, blueprint units via Irene+writer sign-off, and out-of-scope units remain as audited Declined nodes with verbatim rationale. Evaluates without mutating the plan and emits a machine-readable JSON artifact for step-13 auditing.

nodes.name: evaluate_quinn_r_two_branch_gate
nodes.summary: Core gate evaluator that walks every plan unit, routes it down the produced-asset-quality branch or the blueprint sign-off branch, preserves prior declined rationales, and returns a QuinnRTwoBranchResult with per-unit verdicts and an overall pass flag.

nodes.name: emit_quinn_r_gate_artifact
nodes.summary: Writes the serialized gate result JSON to a resolved repo-relative output root, creating parent directories and returning the repo-relative artifact path.

nodes.name: QuinnRUnitVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing a single plan-unit gate verdict: which branch evaluated it, whether it passed, the reason, and the referenced asset.

nodes.name: QuinnRTwoBranchResult
nodes.summary: Top-level gate result model bundling the plan reference, timezone-aware evaluation timestamp, overall pass flag, per-unit verdicts, and carried-forward prior declined rationales.

nodes.name: retrieval_narration_grammar.py
nodes.summary: Pure wording seam (Story 30-5) that turns a Tracy-style research posture result into a single Maya-facing Marcus sentence, without dispatching retrieval or mutating lesson-plan state.

nodes.name: render_retrieval_narration
nodes.summary: Validates a Tracy posture result mapping, normalizes the posture, and renders the corresponding Marcus-facing narration sentence, raising RetrievalNarrationError on malformed input.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Lesson Plan schema primitives (Story 31-1): the Pydantic v2 contract every downstream story reads and writes against — LessonPlan, PlanUnit, ScopeDecision state machine, BlueprintSignoff, gap/dial models, and Irene fit-report shapes with optimistic-revision guards.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Pydantic v2 idioms: field/model validators enforcing timezone-aware datetimes, closed-enum red-rejection, verbatim free-text preservation, and audit-safe dumps of internal-only fields.

nodes.name: PlanRef
nodes.summary: Lightweight reference to a specific lesson plan state via revision number and content digest, used to pin downstream artifacts to the exact plan they evaluated.

nodes.name: Dials
nodes.summary: Per-unit tuning surface holding the enrichment and corroboration dial settings the operator adjusts during the 4A conversation.

nodes.name: IdentifiedGap
nodes.summary: Model describing a diagnosed content gap on a plan unit with a stable gap id, description, and suggested research posture.

nodes.name: ScopeDecision
nodes.summary: State machine for a plan unit's scope ratification: tracks state/scope, the two-level proposed-by actor surface (public Maya-facing vs internal), and enforces legal transitions plus locked-requires-ratification invariants via transition_to and validators.

nodes.name: BlueprintSignoff
nodes.summary: Sign-off record for blueprint units pairing the blueprint asset path with the signoff artifact path, Irene-review and writer-signoff completion flags, and a timezone-aware signature timestamp with repo-relative path validation.

nodes.name: PlanUnit
nodes.summary: The atomic lesson-plan unit binding an event type, source-fitness diagnosis, scope decision, weather band, modality reference, verbatim rationale, gaps, and dials — with validators rejecting red fitness and enforcing scope/state consistency.

nodes.name: LessonPlan
nodes.summary: Root aggregate for the lesson plan: schema version, learning model, structure, plan units, and an optimistic-concurrency revision+digest pair maintained through bump_revision and apply_revision (which raises StaleRevisionError on conflicts).

nodes.name: FitDiagnosis
nodes.summary: Irene's per-unit source-fitness verdict with commentary, a recommended scope decision, and a recommended weather band; red recommendations are rejected at validation time.

nodes.name: FitReport
nodes.summary: Container for a full Irene fitness assessment run: source and plan references, the list of per-unit diagnoses, a timezone-aware generation timestamp, and the Irene time budget.

nodes.name: step_05_pre_packet_handoff.py
nodes.summary: Step 05 pre-packet handoff consumer boundary (Story 30-4): validates the fanout envelope shape and asserts the referenced lesson plan revision is still fresh before the step proceeds.

nodes.name: Step05PrePacketEnvelope
nodes.summary: Envelope contract for step 05 carrying the lesson plan revision, digest, and step id that the consumer validates on receipt.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Consumer entry point: model-validates the incoming envelope into Step05PrePacketEnvelope and asserts plan freshness against the log before handing off.

nodes.name: step_06_plan_lock_fanout.py
nodes.summary: Step 06 plan-lock fanout consumer boundary (Story 30-4): validates the fanout envelope and asserts lesson-plan freshness before the plan-lock fanout step consumes it.

nodes.name: Step06PlanLockFanoutEnvelope
nodes.summary: Envelope contract for step 06 carrying lesson plan revision, digest, and step id for freshness verification at the consumer boundary.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Consumer entry point: model-validates the envelope into Step06PlanLockFanoutEnvelope and asserts the referenced plan revision is fresh.

nodes.name: step_07_gap_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Step 07 gap-dispatch consumer boundary (Story 30-4): validates a gap-dispatch envelope — including unit id, gap type, and bridge status — and asserts lesson-plan freshness before dispatch.

nodes.name: Step07GapDispatchEnvelope
nodes.summary: Envelope contract for step 07 extending the plan revision/digest/step-id triple with unit_id, gap_type, and bridge_status for per-gap dispatch.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Consumer entry point: model-validates the envelope into Step07GapDispatchEnvelope and asserts plan freshness before the gap dispatch proceeds.

nodes.name: trends_projection.py
nodes.summary: W3 produce-time projection of research-packet rows into a trends + hot-topics workbook backmatter brief. Every trend claim and hot-topic callout must be grounded in wrangled packet rows — model-prior-only topics are rejected or the brief is marked unusable rather than fabricated.

nodes.name: ResearchTrendClaim
nodes.summary: Model for a single grounded trend claim carrying the claim text, citation and source identifiers, confidence, evidence-hierarchy tier, peer-review flag, provenance, triangulation status, and reliability score.

nodes.name: HotTopicCallout
nodes.summary: Model for a bounded hot-topic callout with topic, rationale, supporting citation ids, source refs, and confidence.

nodes.name: ResearchTrendsBrief
nodes.summary: Aggregate brief holding projected trends, hot topics, known losses, and an empty_reason, with a usable property that reports whether any grounded content survived projection.

nodes.name: reject_model_prior_topic
nodes.summary: Grounding guard for injected topics: only admits a hot-topic callout when the topic matches wrangled packet-row titles, otherwise rejects it as model-prior-only so no ungrounded topic reaches the workbook.

nodes.name: project_trends_from_packet
nodes.summary: Main projector: converts wrangled research-packet entries into bounded, confidence-sorted trend claims and hot-topic callouts, recording per-entry losses and returning an unusable brief with an empty_reason when nothing grounded survives.

nodes.name: trends_inputs_from_run
nodes.summary: Run-dir convenience wrapper that resolves the research packet for the trends projector from a run directory and delegates to project_trends_from_packet.

nodes.name: render_trends_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a ResearchTrendsBrief into the markdown backmatter section, stripping inline HTML comments and listing trends with citations plus hot-topic callouts.

nodes.name: workbook_producer.py
nodes.summary: Workbook producer for the braid client deliverable — the 4th concrete ModalityProducer. Composes the workbook companion to the narrated deck from transcript segments, exercises, citations, research backmatter, and glossary; markdown is canonical and DOCX is rendered from the same composed model, with numeric/citation/exercise fidelity gates guarding grounding.
nodes.languageNotes: DOCX output is byte-determinism-pinned by rewriting the zip archive with fixed timestamps so repeat renders of the same model hash identically.

nodes.name: TranscriptSegment
nodes.summary: Model for one storyboard transcript segment: segment id, narration text, associated visual file (relative and absolute), visual references, and source ref.

nodes.name: ResearchEntry
nodes.summary: Citation-grade research supplement entry with provider identity, source hash, supported segment, evidence-hierarchy tier, peer-review flag, provenance chain, triangulation status, and reliability score.

nodes.name: WorkbookSidecar
nodes.summary: Result sidecar bundling the produced asset with markdown/DOCX paths and the audit metadata emitted alongside a workbook production run.

nodes.name: WorkbookProducer
nodes.summary: The ModalityProducer implementation: produce() composes the workbook document, renders markdown and DOCX, verifies segment coverage and that the workbook narrative is a superset of the VO transcript, and returns a WorkbookSidecar with produced-asset metadata.

nodes.name: load_transcript_segments
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the storyboard segment manifest from disk into TranscriptSegment models, failing loudly on missing or malformed manifest entries.

nodes.name: assert_unique_collateral_ids
nodes.summary: Guard that scans the collateral spec for duplicate exercise/answer-key ids and raises DuplicateCollateralIdError when any id collides.

nodes.name: audit_workbook_numeric_fidelity
nodes.summary: G1 numeric fidelity gate: extracts numerals from the workbook body and verifies each is grounded in the source text or declared research supplements, raising WorkbookFidelityError on ungrounded numbers.

nodes.name: audit_citation_fidelity
nodes.summary: Verifies every workbook citation resolves against the source-ref manifest so no citation points at a source that was never wrangled.

nodes.name: assert_exercise_fidelity
nodes.summary: Guard asserting the collateral spec's exercises meet the workbook fidelity contract before composition proceeds.

nodes.name: assert_learning_objective_bindings
nodes.summary: Verifies every collateral item that claims a learning-objective binding references a real objective id from the provided learning objectives, failing loudly on dangling bindings.

nodes.name: compose_workbook
nodes.summary: The central composition function: assembles the full workbook document model from plan unit, collateral spec, transcript segments, revoiced prose, learning objectives, answer keys, further reading, research entries, glossary, trends backmatter, and pre-work — honoring encounter mode and render profile.

nodes.name: render_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the composed workbook document model to canonical markdown — the substrate the citation-injection stage fills.

nodes.name: render_docx_from_model
nodes.summary: Renders the client-deliverable DOCX from the same composed document model via python-docx, then pins the resulting zip archive for byte determinism.

nodes.name: dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator-side dispatch seam routing Intake-originated pre-packet envelopes and orchestrator events into the Lesson Plan log; the sole caller of write_api.emit_pre_packet_snapshot for Intake-originated flows, enforcing the single-writer rule.

nodes.name: dispatch_intake_pre_packet
nodes.summary: Routes an Intake-built pre_packet_snapshot envelope into the Lesson Plan log by calling write_api.emit_pre_packet_snapshot with the orchestrator writer identity — the single sanctioned path for Intake-originated emissions.

nodes.name: dispatch_orchestrator_event
nodes.summary: Generic orchestrator event dispatch: validates and appends orchestrator-originated events (scope decisions, plan lock, fanout) to the Lesson Plan log under the single-writer discipline.

nodes.name: fanout.py
nodes.summary: Plan-lock fanout emitter seam (Story 30-4): builds step 05+ envelopes from a locked lesson plan — including per-unit gap envelopes bridged through Tracy results — and emits fanout.envelope.emitted log events through a caller-injected dispatch callable under the single-writer discipline.

nodes.name: StepFanoutEnvelope
nodes.summary: Envelope model produced by plan-lock fanout carrying the target step id, plan revision/digest, and gap-specific fields for downstream step consumers.

nodes.name: emit_plan_lock_fanout
nodes.summary: Main fanout emitter: derives step 05/06/07 envelopes from the locked plan (bridging identified gaps through Tracy results), dispatches each as a fanout.envelope.emitted event, and returns the fanout result summary.

nodes.name: hil_intake.py
nodes.summary: Marcus HIL intake callable (Story 32-5): bridges the conversational Step 04A scope-ratification phase with the programmatic FourALoop.run_4a() call by replaying operator-pre-collected decisions, plus a confirmed-only lesson-plan log reset utility.

nodes.name: MarcusPreCollectedIntakeCallable
nodes.summary: Callable object that replays operator-pre-collected scope decisions into the 4A loop per unit id, raising MissingIntakeDecisionError when a unit has no collected decision.

nodes.name: build_hil_intake_callable
nodes.summary: Factory that validates a mapping of unit-id to scope decision and wraps it in a MarcusPreCollectedIntakeCallable ready to be wired into the workflow runner.

nodes.name: reset_lesson_plan_log
nodes.summary: Destructive-but-gated utility that clears a bundle's lesson-plan log for a fresh 4A run; requires an explicit confirm flag and raises LogResetNotConfirmedError otherwise.

nodes.name: loop.py
nodes.summary: The 4A conversation loop (Story 30-3a): iterates plan units collecting Maya's scope decisions via an intake callable, supports dial tuning and synchronous Irene reassessment with Tracy-backed narration, and triggers plan lock when every unit is ratified.

nodes.name: FourAState
nodes.summary: Mutable loop state holding the current lesson plan and dispatch context, with ratified_units/pending_units views that drive loop progression and lock readiness.

nodes.name: FourALoop
nodes.summary: Loop driver whose run_4a() walks pending plan units, invokes the injected intake callable for each scope decision, emits plan_unit.created events, and locks the plan once all units are ratified.

nodes.name: intake_scope_decision
nodes.summary: Applies one operator scope decision (with verbatim rationale) to a plan unit, transitions its ScopeDecision state, bumps the plan revision, and emits the scope_decision.set event through dispatch.

nodes.name: tune_unit_dials
nodes.summary: Adjusts a plan unit's enrichment/corroboration dials during the 4A conversation, bumping the plan revision and emitting the corresponding log event.

nodes.name: sync_reassess_with_irene
nodes.summary: Synchronous Irene reassessment path: re-diagnoses the source against the plan (honoring p95 and budget thresholds), folds in Tracy retrieval results with Maya-facing narration, carries prior declined rationales forward, and returns a SyncReassessmentResult.

nodes.name: trigger_plan_lock_if_ready
nodes.summary: Checks whether every plan unit is ratified and, if so, locks the plan and emits plan.locked — raising PlanAlreadyLockedError when a newer locked revision already exists.

nodes.name: maya_walkthrough.py
nodes.summary: Maya journey walkthrough (Story 32-4): drives the landed MVP chain through a canned 7-page SME fixture and records stage-by-stage evidence for the pantomime AC — paste source, weather ribbon, gray card click, Marcus delegation, one-sentence rationale, card turns gold. Release-gate ceremony only; routes no user-facing traffic.

nodes.name: MayaWalkthroughResult
nodes.summary: In-module result container aggregating the six per-stage evidence models plus run metadata and a timezone-aware timestamp for the walkthrough report.

nodes.name: run_maya_walkthrough
nodes.summary: Entry point that executes all six pantomime stages against the fixture — pasting source, verifying the weather ribbon, clicking a gray card, proposing delegation, entering the operator rationale, and confirming the card turned gold — and writes the evidence artifact.

nodes.name: trial_smoke_harness.py
nodes.summary: Trial-run smoke harness (Story 32-3): exercises the packet-to-plan-lock chain end to end — intake handoff, 4A workflow, fanout step consumers, Quinn-R gate — and emits a TrialSmokeReport with per-step statuses and a trial-readiness battery.

nodes.name: TrialReadinessBattery
nodes.summary: Model recording the seven trial-readiness checks the harness evaluates before declaring a bundle ready for a tracked trial run.

nodes.name: TrialSmokeReport
nodes.summary: Top-level smoke report bundling run id, ordered step results, overall pass flag, failure detail, and the readiness battery.

nodes.name: run_trial_run_smoke_harness
nodes.summary: Executes the full smoke sequence against a bundle dir: builds a packet plan, routes it through the Step 4A workflow with canned decisions, consumes the step 05/06/07 fanout envelopes, evaluates the Quinn-R gate, and assembles the TrialSmokeReport with fail-fast failure detail.

nodes.name: workflow_runner.py
nodes.summary: Lesson Planner workflow runner seam (Story 32-1): inserts the Step 4A conversation between workflow steps and routes the step-04 gate into step 05, accepting pre-collected operator decisions (Story 32-5) so the production HIL intake callable can drive ratification.

nodes.name: insert_between
nodes.summary: List surgery helper that inserts a new workflow step between two named step ids, validating both anchors exist and are adjacent.

nodes.name: route_step_04_gate_to_step_05
nodes.summary: Routes the step-04 gate outcome into step 05 by running the 4A loop over the packet plan with the injected (or pre-collected) intake callable, facade, prior declined rationales, and optional Tracy bridge, returning the Step4AWorkflowResult.

nodes.name: write_api.py
nodes.summary: Single-writer entry point for Marcus-Orchestrator writes to the Lesson Plan log: the one sanctioned surface for appending pre_packet_snapshot events, enforcing the writer-identity invariant at entry with a caller-scoped UnauthorizedFacadeCallerError.

nodes.name: UnauthorizedFacadeCallerError
nodes.summary: Error raised when a caller other than the sanctioned orchestrator writer attempts to append to the Lesson Plan log, with a caller-scoped message explaining the single-writer rule.

nodes.name: emit_pre_packet_snapshot
nodes.summary: Type-gates the pre-validated EventEnvelope, verifies the orchestrator writer identity, and appends the pre_packet_snapshot event to the Lesson Plan log — the single sanctioned write path for this event type.

nodes.name: append_event
nodes.summary: Thin last-mile append helper that writes a validated event into the lesson-plan log state under the writer-identity check.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: The 07W specialist act: a deterministic in-graph adapter over WorkbookProducer that self-resolves inputs from the running run directory — segment manifest, corpus, Irene's authored collateral blueprint, research/glossary/trends/pre-work projections — reconciles workbook-brief authority, produces the workbook, and threads sidecar refs into the production envelope.

nodes.name: WorkbookInputs
nodes.summary: Wide input bundle (24 fields) aggregating everything build_workbook_inputs resolves from the run dir — plan unit, spec, segments, objectives, answer keys, research, glossary, trends, pre-work, and render options — ready for compose/produce.

nodes.name: decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Decodes the dispatched payload from the run state per the Ratchet-D payload contract, failing loudly when required keys are missing or malformed.

nodes.name: build_workbook_inputs
nodes.summary: The heavy resolver: reads the run dir to assemble WorkbookInputs — transcript segments mapped to Gary deck exports, G1 source text, Irene's collateral spec, learning-objective overlays, research/glossary/trends projections, figure supplements, and pre-work — honoring the validated brief's authority.

nodes.name: produce_workbook
nodes.summary: Runs the WorkbookProducer over the assembled inputs, writes the markdown and DOCX artifacts plus sidecar into the run dir, and returns the sidecar and economics for envelope threading.

nodes.name: _reconcile_workbook_brief_authority
nodes.summary: Reconciles which workbook brief governs the production — the persisted production envelope's validated brief versus state-carried payloads — so the produce step honors the operator-approved authority chain.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Graph-node entry point: decodes the payload, resolves the run dir, reconciles brief authority, builds inputs, produces the workbook, and returns the updated state with sidecar refs and trail entries — or a skip output when preconditions are absent.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Ratchet-D consumed-payload vocabulary for the 07W workbook producer: documents that the terminal sidecar self-resolves inputs from the run dir via state.run_id and declares the single resolvable trigger dependency (upstream_output) it actually reads from the dispatched payload.

layers.name: Marcus Orchestration
layers.description: Runtime orchestrator: Marcus-SPOC CLI, production runner, LangGraph workflow wiring, lesson-plan authoring, course-source handling, and styleguide resolution under app/marcus

layers.name: Specialist Agents & Composers
layers.description: 9-node scaffold specialist agents (Irene, Gary, Texas, workbook_producer, ...) under app/specialists plus the section composers under app/composers

layers.name: Gates & Workflow Substrate
layers.description: HIL gates G1/G2C/G3/G4 and decision cards, pipeline-manifest compiler and gate topology, parity/lockstep contracts, Cora block-mode story workflow, and replay regression harness

layers.name: Domain Models
layers.description: Pydantic v2 state and contract models under app/models: run state, envelopes, learning events, JSON Schemas, and shape-pinned data contracts

layers.name: Runtime & Transport
layers.description: Runtime persistence and checkpointing, append-only ledger with SQL schema, audit chain, HTTP gate endpoint, MCP server, and the runtime server entry point

layers.name: Operator & Developer Tooling
layers.description: Operator and developer scripts (scripts/utilities, generators, api_clients, retrieval), flight-deck HUD, notify daemon, smoke tests, and diagnostics

layers.name: BMAD Agent Skills
layers.description: skills/bmad-agent-* persona SKILL.md files, references, and helper scripts for Marcus, Texas, CD, and the other custom BMAD agents

layers.name: Project Root & Configuration
layers.description: Root-level project configuration: environment template, git attributes, coverage artifact, and tooling ignore lists

tour.title: Marcus-SPOC Operator Surface
tour.description: Everything begins at `python -m app.marcus.cli`, an argparse dispatcher that wires the trial, gate, adhoc, plan-dialogue, and plan-ratify subcommands into one front door. Behind it sits marcus_spoc.py — the conversational single-point-of-contact that narrates each gate in persona (sources, ingestion, lesson plan, research, treatment, voice, G0 enrichment) and runs ceremonies like the styleguide pick-commit. The facade enforces the binding voice register (first person, present tense, no hedges) so every operator-visible string sounds like one Marcus, hiding the orchestrator/persona duality split behind a single surface.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `python -m package` convention runs a package's __main__.py as a script. Combined with argparse subcommands, one entry point cleanly fans out to many CLI verbs without separate executables.

tour.title: The Deterministic Neck: Manifest Compiler
tour.description: The pipeline is not hand-wired — it is compiled from a declarative manifest. schema.py defines the Pydantic-v2 contract (NodeSpec, EdgeSpec, ProjectionSpec, PipelineManifest) with cross-field validators for unique node ids, entrypoint resolution, and edge integrity, while compiler.py validates gate codes, model-config refs, conditions, decision-card schemas, dependency cycles, and the frozen graph version before assembling everything into a LangGraph StateGraph. gate_topology.py can render or audit-diff the resulting gate topology, which is how the 'frozen-graph reproducibility ceremony' from the README is enforced in code.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 model_validator(mode='after') hooks let a schema enforce cross-field invariants (like 'every edge endpoint must be a declared node') at parse time — the manifest is rejected before any graph is built, making invalid pipelines unrepresentable.

tour.title: Production Orchestration Walk
tour.description: production_runner.py is the composition layer with the highest fan-out in the codebase (49 imports): it drives the full trial lifecycle — start, resume, batch-resume, and recover walks — plus specialist dispatch with retry, HIL gate pauses, envelope persistence, and cost recording. A key gotcha lives here: there are TWO node walks (the start walk stops at G1), so gate-pause side-effects must land in both. workflow_runner.py is the Lesson Planner seam that inserts the Step 4A conversation between workflow steps, and gate_runner.py guards the Marcus duality boundary with calibration tripwires.

tour.title: Domain Models & Run State
tour.description: The two most-depended-upon files in the entire graph live here: _base.py (fan-in 58) supplies the shared Pydantic-v2 idioms every state model uses, and run_state.py (fan-in 44) defines RunState, the single object threaded through the compiled graph. SpecialistEnvelope is the request/response wrapper around one specialist invocation, and SpecialistReturn is the payload a specialist hands back to the orchestrator — together they are the 'extraordinarily robust carrier' contract: schema-validated, versioned, additive-safe, and fail-loud. Understanding these four files unlocks nearly every other module in the tour.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic's `model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True, validate_assignment=True)` turns models into tamper-resistant value objects — mutation attempts raise instead of silently corrupting run state that must survive checkpointing and resume.

tour.title: Decision Cards & Operator Verdicts
tour.description: When the pipeline pauses at a gate, it presents the operator a typed DecisionCard: _base.py defines the shared card families and per-gate modules like g1.py pin each gate's exact shape (the graph carries cards from G0 through G6). The operator's answer becomes a frozen OperatorVerdict value object with UUID4, timezone-aware timestamps, and a sha256 tamper-evidence digest — the verdict is cryptographically auditable, not just a boolean. The JSON schema in app/gates/schema/ is the emitted contract that external surfaces validate against.

tour.title: HIL Gate Substrate
tour.description: Gates are implemented as LangGraph interrupts: the graph literally suspends until a human verdict arrives. resume_api.py (fan-in 34 — nearly every specialist binds to it) stores the pending DecisionCard and exposes the resume path that feeds the operator's verdict back into the paused graph; verdict.py re-exports the verdict types with import-time guardrails, and party_mode_as_interrupt.py shows the pattern generalized — even a multi-agent party review is just another interrupt. Building on Step 5, this is where a frozen verdict actually un-pauses the machine.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph's interrupt() primitive checkpoints graph state and returns control to the caller; resuming with a Command(resume=value) re-enters the exact node with the human's input — human-in-the-loop as a first-class control-flow construct rather than a polling hack.

tour.title: The 9-Node Specialist Scaffold
tour.description: Every specialist (Irene, Gary, Vera, Quinn-R, the new workbook_producer...) is stamped from one canonical scaffold: graph.py defines the 9-node walk (_receive, _plan, _act, and companions), state.py pins the state shape, and contract.py provides ScaffoldConformanceResult — a machine check that a concrete specialist actually conforms to the scaffold. dispatch_errors.py (fan-in 38) defines the shared failure vocabulary so every specialist fails the same way. This uniformity is why the manifest compiler from Step 2 can wire any specialist into the graph sight-unseen.

tour.title: Concrete Specialists: Irene & Gary
tour.description: Two flagship instantiations of the scaffold. Irene's Pass-2 graph is the largest specialist module: it assembles a byte-stable narration prompt and enforces an extensive grounding regime — figure-fidelity assertions, reading-path conformance, warm-callback attachment, voice-direction annotation (the protected VO-to-on-screen alignment invariant lives here). Her payload_contract.py freezes exactly which envelope keys the act body may read, asserted by a manifest lockstep test. Gary's graph decodes the envelope, dispatches slide generation through the thin gamma_dispatch.py seam (live executor or deterministic fixture), and binds his gate decision to the resume API from Step 6.

tour.title: Lesson Plan Compilation
tour.description: Source material and learning objectives are KING in this system, and this package is where they become a governed plan. schema.py (fan-in 18) is the Pydantic contract every downstream story reads: LessonPlan, PlanUnit, the ScopeDecision state machine, and blueprint sign-off shapes with optimistic-revision guards. learning_objective.py is the single source of truth for the provenance-anchored, lifecycle-aware LearningObjective entity, planning_context.py loads ratified LOs and assesses corpus coverage fail-loud, and coverage_manifest.py audits that every envelope surface along the 05-to-13 path preserves the plan revision and digest — plan freshness is verified, not assumed.

tour.title: Workbook Substrate (Epics 36-40)
tour.description: The newest production band — landed in the 2026-07 workbook arc and live-gate PASSED on 2026-07-15. workbook_wiring.py is the deterministic orchestration seam for the four-node 07W band: it resolves the slide authority map under a dispatch lock, composes the Deep Dive with journaled exactly-once provider dispatch, builds the workbook brief, and reconciles activated artifacts into the production envelope. The workbook_producer specialist's _act is a deterministic in-graph adapter that self-resolves its inputs from the run directory (segment manifest, corpus, Irene's blueprint, research/glossary/trends/pre-work projections), while lesson_plan/workbook_producer.py is the underlying 4th ModalityProducer where markdown is canonical and DOCX renders from the same composed model. The 07W Jinja2 template is the pack-side face of the same step.
tour.languageLesson: The exactly-once journal pattern: before dispatching a costly live LLM/provider call, write an intent record; on crash-resume, replay the journal instead of re-dispatching. This makes expensive side-effecting nodes idempotent across resume walks.

tour.title: Research, Ask-A & G0 Live Enrichment
tour.description: Enrichment feeds the workbook with grounded knowledge through three wired paths. g0_enrichment_wiring.py is the runner-wired G0 pre-pass: it fingerprints the corpus, splits files into typed components (with a live LLM extraction leg and salvage parsing), extracts provisional learning objectives, and freezes the result behind frozen artifact models complete with anti-anchoring sidecars — independent parse, operator merge, and a dissent ledger. ask_a_research_wiring.py performs exactly-once Ask-A research dispatch at node 07W.2 with crash-safe journal replay, credibility-classifying provider rows against the strict contracts in ask_a_enrichment.py. research_wiring.py threads the Irene-to-Tracy-to-Texas research bridge through the production runner so research goals yield cited entries carrying source_ref and source_hash for the workbook producer.

tour.title: Workbook Projections & Quinn-R Gate
tour.description: Enriched knowledge only counts if the deliverable is genuinely shaped by it. workbook_enrichment.py projects the frozen, operator-confirmed G0 card (typed components, citations, pedagogy annotations) into workbook-producer inputs so further-reading links and exercise Bloom levels come from the enriched corpus, while prework_projection.py supplies strict deterministic scene/friction/promise contracts and workbook_prework_writers.py provides the live structured-output 07W.1 semantic writers with typed degradation paths when provider output fails its gates. Quality is closed by Quinn-R: quinn_r_gate.py is the deterministic step-13 two-branch gate (produced-asset quality vs Irene+writer sign-off) that evaluates without mutating the plan, backed by the quinn_r specialist graph stamped from Step 7's scaffold.

tour.title: Runtime, Persistence & Ledger
tour.description: Underneath everything sits the Slab-1 runtime substrate. checkpointer.py yields an AsyncPostgresSaver so LangGraph checkpoints persist to Postgres — this is what makes the gate pauses from Step 6 survivable across process restarts. compiled_graph_digest.py computes a deterministic two-part digest (manifest + dispatch snapshot) and asserts they match, detecting compilation drift against the frozen graph from Step 2. Every gate event and operator verdict is also appended to the tamper-evident learning ledger: schema.sql defines the ledger_events table and emitter.py writes to it, giving the system an audit trail independent of run state.
tour.languageLesson: An append-only event table (no UPDATEs, indexed by run and event type) is the SQL shape of an audit ledger: tamper-evidence comes from never rewriting history, and queries reconstruct state by replaying events.

tour.title: Cora Dev-Graph & Governance
tour.description: The production pipeline has a sibling: Cora, the dev-graph that governs changes to the pipeline itself. graph.py loads and compiles Cora's own manifest (the same compiled-manifest philosophy as Step 2, aimed at story work), with handlers for plan/implement/test/review/close story phases. block_mode_node.py is the in-graph guard that enforces the pipeline lockstep regime — edits to manifest-trigger paths are blocked until governance clears them — and the BMAD skill file is how the Cora persona activates in an agent session. The lesson of the whole tour: in this codebase, even the development process is a compiled, gated graph.
